---
title: "Rapid intensification of coral bleaching on the Great Barrier Reef"
date: "2026-01-28"
editor: visual
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
execute:
  eval: true
  echo: false
  cache: false
---


Anthropogenic warming has made marine heatwaves more frequent, intense and prolonged, and coral reefs are among the most impacted ecosystems across the globe. On the Great Barrier Reef, mass coral bleaching was unrecorded before 1998 but has since recurred with accelerating frequency, with major events in 1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024 and 2025, the six most recent falling within a single decade. Whether this escalation reflects increasing thermal stress or a shift in the response of reefs to warming has been difficult to resolve in the absence of long-term consistent records of thermal stress and a correspondingly long record of biological response.

Here we draw on an exceptional record of reef-scale responses to coral bleaching: standardised aerial bleaching surveys spanning the full length of the Great Barrier Reef, comprising ~ 5,500 reef-level observations spanning 1,826 individual reefs across the eight major bleaching events of the past 28 years. Few coral-reef systems anywhere have been surveyed so consistently, at this spatial extent, over so many successive events, and the dataset uniquely allows the severity of bleaching to be tracked both across the reef and within individual reefs through repeated exposure.

Resolving long-term patterns of thermal stress is challenging, as satellite records are limited by the accuracy and consistency. To place the modern events in their full context we therefore reconstructed a continuous 85-year SST record (1940–2025) by bias-correcting the ERA5 reanalysis against observed OISST using seasonally stratified, anomaly-based quantile mapping, yielding a single homogeneous time series that reproduces the observed climatology and variability while extending four decades before OISST satellite era. This reconstruction provides a physically consistent pre-1981 baseline for the Great Barrier Reef at 1/4° spatial resolution, facilitating insight into spatiotemporal trends in SST anomalies and DHW spanning 1941-2026 

### Rise of ocean warming and thermal anomalies

Reef-wide sea-surface temperature on the Great Barrier Reef rose substantially across the 85-year reconstruction (Figure 1a). Rather than warming gradually throughout the record, the annual mean SST series is best described by an abrupt change in regime: Bayesian changepoint detection identifies a single dominant breakpoint near 1998, with no significant trend across the preceding six decades and a sustained linear increase from 1998 onward (Table S1). Betweeen 1940-200, the mean SST on the GBR existd in a fluctuating but approximately stationary thermal state (Figure 1a) before transitioning into a period of persistent warming that continues to the present.

The sea-surface temperature anomaly tracks this transition closely. Prior to 2000 the anomaly distribution sits close to zero, spanning  −0.3 to +0.5 °C and fluctuating with ENSO Cycles (Figure Sx), whereas after 2000 it shifts to a persistently positive range, commonly +0.2 to +1.0 °C (Figure 1a). As with SST, the anomaly series is better characterised by a step change around 1998 than by a continuous trend (Table S1), reinforcing the interpretation of a regime shift rather than slow drift. This positive anomaly is not distributed evenly across the reef but is spatially structured: warming rates (°C yr-1) are strengthening toward the northern Great Barrier Reef and tapering southward, so that the northern sectors have warmed both earliest and fastest (Figure 1b). From a cross-shelf perspective, the outer-reef and coral sea borders are warming at a x2 rate than inshore reefs across the central and southern sectors (Figure 1b)


### Shift from episodic to recurrent and stronger heatwaves (DHW)

Accumulated thermal stress, expressed as Degree Heating Weeks, shows the same regime change as the underlying temperature. The early part of the record is dominated by near-zero DHW years interrupted only by occasional moderate spikes, indicating a reef that experienced acute heat stress rarely and at low intensity (Figure S2). From the late 1990s this pattern breaks down, and high-DHW years become both more frequent and more intense, marking a transition from episodic to recurrent thermal stress (Figure 1a). The most extreme years in the reconstruction coincide with the eight mass-bleaching events analysed here (1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025), and the clustering of severe years has tightened over time, with four of the last five years ranking among the most severe in the entire 85-year record.

This intensification is also spatially organised: maximum DHW increases northward and exceeds 20 °C-weeks in the far north, mirroring the latitudinal gradient seen in the warming rate (Figure 1c). For most of the reef the single hottest year on record now falls within the last five years, with the principal exceptions confined to parts of the central and southern inshore reef where peak stress occurred earlier (Figure 1d). Aggregating the record into decades makes the acceleration explicit: the number of years exceeding 6 °C-weeks rises from one to three per decade through 1984–2015 to a sharp intensification in 2016–2025, when severe thermal-stress years became concentrated across the northern and central Great Barrier Reef (Figure 1e). Together these patterns describe a system in which damaging heat has shifted from a rare, localised occurrence to a frequent and spatially extensive feature of the modern GBR reef system.

#### Figure 1

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: false
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-cap: |
#|   **Figure 1 | Eight decades of intensifying thermal stress on the Great
#|   Barrier Reef (1940–2025).**
#|   (**a**) Annual maximum Degree Heating Weeks (DHW, °C-weeks) across the Great
#|   Barrier Reef from a reconstructed 85-year record (OISST2, 1940–2025), bar
#|   colour scaled to DHW. Vertical shaded bars mark the eight mass-bleaching
#|   events analysed (1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025). High-DHW
#|   years were episodic before the late 1990s and have become both more frequent
#|   and more intense since, with the most extreme values concentrated in the last
#|   decade. *Insets:* reef-wide mean (i) sea-surface temperature (SST, °C) and
#|   (ii) sea-surface temperature anomaly (SSTA, °C), 1940–2025; horizontal lines
#|   and shaded bands show the mean and variability of the pre- and
#|   post-breakpoint periods identified by Bayesian changepoint detection (green,
#|   stationary regime to ~1998; red, warming regime thereafter), with the fitted
#|   post-breakpoint trend in dark red. SST shifts from a stationary regime to
#|   sustained warming, and SSTA from centred on zero to persistently positive,
#|   around 1998. (**b**) Rate of SSTA increase per decade (°C decade⁻¹; OISST
#|   1982–2024) by location; warming is strongest in the northern Great Barrier
#|   Reef. (**c**) Maximum DHW (DHWmax, °C-weeks; OISST 1982–2024) recorded at each
#|   location over the satellite record, exceeding 20 °C-weeks in the north.
#|   (**d**) Year in which each location experienced its maximum DHW; for most of
#|   the reef the hottest year on record falls within the last decade. (**e**)
#|   Number of years exceeding 6 °C-weeks DHW per decade across four successive
#|   decades (1986–1995, 1996–2005, 2006–2015, 2016–2025), showing a marked
#|   increase in the frequency of severe thermal-stress years in the most recent
#|   decade, concentrated in the northern and central Great Barrier Reef.


Sys.setenv("LWGEOM_WKT" = "true")
sf::sf_use_s2()


library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(dhw)
library(terra)
library(Rbeast)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(tibble)
library(tidyr)
library(ggridges)
library(units)

### load all datasets:
GBR_CRW_SST <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_CoralTemp_SST.tif")
GBR_CRW_DHW <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_CoralTemp_DHW.tif")

GBR_OISST_SST <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST_SST.tif")
GBR_OISST_SSTA <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST_SSTA.tif")
GBR_OISST_DHW <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST_DHW.tif")

GBR_OISST2_SST <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST2_SST.tif")
GBR_OISST2_SSTA <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST2_SSTA.tif")
GBR_OISST2_DHW <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST2_DHW.tif")
 
# gbr_reef_outline <- download_gbr_spatial(return="base")
# gbr_reefs <- download_gbr_spatial(return="combined")
# saveRDS(gbr_reef_outline, "/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/data/gbr_reef_outline.rds")
# saveRDS(gbr_reefs, "/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/data/gbr_reefs.rds")

gbr_reef_outline <- readRDS("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/data/gbr_reef_outline.rds")
gbr_reefs <- readRDS("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/data/gbr_reefs.rds")
gbr_reef_all <- readRDS("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/data/gbr_reef_outline.rds")

# manually set GBR_OISST_SST |> st_bbox()
bb <- st_bbox(c(xmin = 141.00, ymin = -24.50, xmax = 152.75, ymax = -9.00), 
              crs = st_crs(4326))  

aus_outline <- gbr_reef_outline |> slice_max(n=1, order_by=SHAPE_AREA)

# ausmap <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale="large", country="Australia") |>
#   st_transform(4326) |>
#   st_crop(bb)
# saveRDS(ausmap, "~/GBR-dhw/data/ausmap.rds")
ausmap <- readRDS("~/GBR-dhw/data/ausmap.rds")


###############################################################################
########## extract SST and SSTA data for Figure 1
###############################################################################

sst_ts <- terra::global(GBR_OISST2_SST, mean, na.rm = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(rownames(.))) |>
  mutate(
    year  = year(date),
    month = month(date),
    austral_year = if_else(month == 12L, year + 1L, year)  # Dec belongs to next year
  ) %>%
  filter(month %in% seq(1:3)) %>%
  filter(austral_year > 1940, austral_year < 2026) %>%
  group_by(austral_year) %>%
  summarise(sst = mean(mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  rename(year = austral_year)

sst_ts <- sst_ts %>% arrange(year)
y_sst <- sst_ts$sst
t_sst <- sst_ts$year
 
beast_sst <- beast(
  y_sst,
  start  = t_sst[1],
  deltat = 1,
  season = "none",
  print.progress = FALSE,
  quiet = TRUE
)

cp_sst   <- beast_sst$trend$cp
cp_sst <- cp_sst
cppr_sst <- beast_sst$trend$cpPr
if (is.null(cppr_sst)) cppr_sst <- beast_sst$trend$cpProb

sst_cp_tbl <- tibble(cp = cp_sst, cp_prob = cppr_sst) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(round(cp))) %>%
  arrange(desc(cp_prob))

breakpoints_sst <- sst_cp_tbl$cp[1:min(2, nrow(sst_cp_tbl))]
breakyears_sst  <- sst_cp_tbl$year[1:min(2, nrow(sst_cp_tbl))]

GBR_OISST2_SSTA <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST2_SSTA.tif")

ssta_ts <- terra::global(GBR_OISST2_SSTA, mean, na.rm = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(rownames(.))) |>
  mutate(
    year  = year(date),
    month = month(date),
    austral_year = if_else(month == 12L, year + 1L, year)  # Dec belongs to next year
  ) %>%
  filter(month %in% c(12, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  filter(austral_year > 1940, austral_year < 2026) %>%
  group_by(austral_year) %>%
  summarise(ssta = mean(mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  rename(year = austral_year)


ssta_ts <- ssta_ts %>% arrange(year)
y_ssta <- ssta_ts$ssta
t_ssta <- ssta_ts$year

beast_ssta <- beast(
  y_ssta,
  start  = t_ssta[1],
  deltat = 1,
  season = "none",
  print.progress = FALSE,
  quiet = TRUE
)

cp_ssta   <- beast_ssta$trend$cp
cppr_ssta <- beast_ssta$trend$cpPr
if (is.null(cppr_ssta)) cppr_ssta <- beast_ssta$trend$cpProb

ssta_cp_tbl <- tibble(cp = cp_ssta, cp_prob = cppr_ssta) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(round(cp))) %>%
  arrange(desc(cp_prob))

breakpoints_ssta <- ssta_cp_tbl$cp[1:min(2, nrow(ssta_cp_tbl))]
breakyears_ssta  <- ssta_cp_tbl$year[1:min(2, nrow(ssta_cp_tbl))]


GBR_OISST2_DHW <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST2_DHW.tif")

sst_dhw <- terra::global(GBR_OISST2_DHW, mean, na.rm = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(rownames(.))) |>
  mutate(
    year  = year(date),
    month = month(date),
    austral_year = if_else(month == 12L, year + 1L, year)  # Dec belongs to next year
  ) %>%
  #filter(month %in% c(12, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  filter(austral_year > 1940, austral_year < 2026) %>%
  group_by(austral_year) %>%
  summarise(dhw = max(mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  rename(year = austral_year)
 

sst_dhw <- sst_dhw %>% arrange(year)
y_dhw <- sst_dhw$dhw
t_dhw <- sst_dhw$year

beast_dhw <- beast(
  y_dhw,
  start  = t_dhw[1],
  deltat = 1,
  season = "none",
  print.progress = FALSE,
  quiet = TRUE
)

cp_dhw   <- beast_dhw$trend$cp
cppr_dhw <- beast_dhw$trend$cpPr
if (is.null(cppr_dhw)) cppr_dhw <- beast_dhw$trend$cpProb

sst_cp_tbl <- tibble(cp = cp_dhw, cp_prob = cppr_dhw) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(round(cp))) %>%
  arrange(desc(cp_prob))

breakpoints_dhw <- sst_cp_tbl$cp[1:min(2, nrow(sst_cp_tbl))]
breakyears_dhw  <- sst_cp_tbl$year[1:min(2, nrow(sst_cp_tbl))]

### fit models to BEAST CPD output
## SST
segment_1_sst <- sst_ts |> filter(year <= (breakpoints_sst[1]))
segment_2_sst <- sst_ts |> filter(year >= (breakpoints_sst[1]))
segment_mean_1_sst <- data.frame(year = c(min(segment_1_sst$year), max(segment_1_sst$year)), sst = rep(mean(segment_1_sst$sst),2))
segment_mean_2_sst <- data.frame(year = c(min(segment_2_sst$year), max(segment_2_sst$year)), sst = rep(mean(segment_2_sst$sst),2))

model_1_sst <- lm(sst ~ year, data = segment_1_sst)
model_2_sst <- lm(sst ~ year, data = segment_2_sst)

segment_1_sst_rect <- segment_1_sst |>
  summarise(
    start_year = min(year),
    end_year = max(year),
    ymin = min(sst),
    ymax = max(sst)
  )

segment_2_sst_rect <- segment_2_sst |>
  summarise(
    start_year = min(year),
    end_year = max(year),
    ymin = min(sst),
    ymax = max(sst)
  )

segment_1_sst_mean <- segment_1_sst |>
  summarise(
    year = mean(year),
    sst = mean(sst)
  )
segment_2_sst_mean <- segment_2_sst |>
  summarise(
    year = mean(year),
    sst = mean(sst)
  )


## SSTA

segment_1_ssta <- ssta_ts |> filter(year <= (breakpoints_ssta[1]))
segment_2_ssta <- ssta_ts |> filter(year >= (breakpoints_ssta[1]))
segment_mean_1_ssta <- data.frame(year = c(min(segment_1_ssta$year), max(segment_1_ssta$year)), ssta = rep(mean(segment_1_ssta$ssta),2))
segment_mean_2_ssta <- data.frame(year = c(min(segment_2_ssta$year), max(segment_2_ssta$year)), ssta = rep(mean(segment_2_ssta$ssta),2))

model_1_ssta <- lm(ssta ~ year, data = segment_1_ssta)
model_2_ssta <- lm(ssta ~ year, data = segment_2_ssta)

segment_1_ssta_rect <- segment_1_ssta |>
  summarise(
    start_year = min(year),
    end_year = max(year),
    ymin = min(ssta),
    ymax = max(ssta)
  )

segment_2_ssta_rect <- segment_2_ssta |>
  summarise(
    start_year = min(year),
    end_year = max(year),
    ymin = min(ssta),
    ymax = max(ssta)
  )

segment_1_ssta_mean <- segment_1_ssta |>
  summarise(
    year = mean(year),
    ssta = mean(ssta)
  )
segment_2_ssta_mean <- segment_2_ssta |>
  summarise(
    year = mean(year),
    ssta = mean(ssta)
  )


###############################################################################
############################## plot main outputs ##############################
###############################################################################

main_dhw <- ggplot() + theme_bw() +
 geom_vline(xintercept=c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025), linewidth=2.8, alpha=0.1, color="coral4") +
 geom_col(data=sst_dhw, aes(x=year, y=dhw, fill=dhw), color="black", linewidth=0.25, show.legend=FALSE) +
 scale_fill_distiller(palette="YlOrRd", direction=1) + 
 #scale_fill_distiller(palette="RdBu", direction=-1) + 
 xlab("") + ylab("Degree Heating Weeks") +
 scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,12), breaks=seq(0,10,2)) + 
 scale_x_continuous(limits=c(1940,2026), breaks=seq(1940,2025,10)) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())  # Drop minor x grids) 


sst_break <- ggplot() + theme_bw() +
  geom_rect(data = segment_1_sst_rect, 
            aes(xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), 
            fill="darkslategray4", alpha=0.1) + 
  geom_rect(data = segment_2_sst_rect, 
            aes(xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), 
            fill="coral2", alpha=0.1) + 
  geom_line(data = segment_1_sst, 
            aes(x = year, y = sst), 
            color="darkgrey", linewidth=0.2) +
  geom_point(data = segment_1_sst, 
             aes(x = year, y = sst), 
             fill="darkslategray4", shape=21, size=1.5, show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_line(data = segment_2_sst, 
            aes(x = year, y = sst), 
            color="darkgrey",linewidth=0.2) +
  geom_point(data = segment_2_sst, 
             aes(x = year, y = sst), 
             fill="coral2", shape=21, size=1.5, show.legend=FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette="RdBu") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = breakpoints_sst[1], color = "darkgrey", linewidth=2, alpha=0.4) +
  geom_smooth(data = segment_2_sst, 
              aes(x = year, y = sst), 
             method = "lm", se = TRUE, linetype="solid", 
             color = "indianred4",  fill = "coral2", linewidth = 0.5, alpha=0.05) +
  geom_line(data=segment_mean_1_sst, aes(x=year, y=sst), linewidth=0.25, linetype="solid") +
  geom_line(data=segment_mean_2_sst, aes(x=year, y=sst), linewidth=0.25, linetype="solid") +
  labs(x = "", y = "SST (°C)") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x=element_blank(),
    axis.text.x=element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
    axis.text=element_text(size=6),
    axis.title=element_text(size=7),
    axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.25, colour = "darkgrey"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 0, fill = "transparent"), 
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()) 


ssta_break <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_rect(
    data = segment_1_ssta_rect,
    aes(xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
   fill="darkslategray4", alpha=0.1
  ) +
  geom_rect(data = segment_2_ssta_rect, 
            aes(xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), 
            fill = "coral2", alpha = 0.1) +
  geom_line(data = segment_1_ssta, aes(x = year, y = ssta), color = "darkgrey", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_point(data = segment_1_ssta, aes(x = year, y = ssta), fill = "darkslategray3", shape = 21, alpha = 1, size = 1.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_line(data = segment_2_ssta, aes(x = year, y = ssta), color = "darkgrey", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_point(data = segment_2_ssta, aes(x = year, y = ssta), fill = "indianred1", shape = 21, alpha = 1, size = 1.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1940, 2026)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.6, 1.2)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = breakpoints_ssta[1], color = "darkgrey", linewidth=2, alpha=0.2) +
  geom_line(data=segment_mean_1_ssta, aes(x=year, y=ssta), linewidth=0.25, linetype="solid") +
  geom_line(data=segment_mean_2_ssta, aes(x=year, y=ssta), linewidth=0.25, linetype="solid") +
  geom_line(data=ssta_ts, aes(x=year, y=ssta), linewidth=0.25, linetype="solid") +
  labs(x = "", y = "SSTA (°C)") +# scale_y_continuous(limits=c(-0.5, 1.5)) +
  theme(
    axis.text=element_text(size=6),
    axis.title=element_text(size=7),
    axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.25, colour = "darkgrey"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 0, fill = "transparent"), # Change border thickness
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(), # Drop minor y grids
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), # Drop major x grids
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 0, l = 10)) 



inset <- sst_break / ssta_break + theme(plot.margin = margin(-1, 1, 0, 0))

df.labels <- data.frame(date=c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025),
                        label=c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025), 
                        dhw = rep(11.8, 8))



###################################################################### 
# Rate of change positive SST Anomlies 1981-2024
######################################################################

# load OISST
library(sf)
library(dhw)

# Define the function to calculate the slope across the entire time series
slope <- terra::app(GBR_OISST_SSTA, function(sst_ts) {
  if (sum(!is.na(sst_ts)) > 1) {  # Ensure there are more than one non-NA values
    df <- data.frame(sst = sst_ts, time = seq_along(sst_ts))  # Create a time series index
    lm_fit <- stats::lm(sst ~ time, data = df)  # Fit linear model (sst as response, time as predictor)
    return(coef(lm_fit)[2])  # Return the slope (the coefficient for time)
  } else {
    return(NA)  # Return NA if not enough data
  }
})

annual_rate_of_change <- slope * 365.25 *10

# Create grid cells covering the raster extent
grid_cells <- st_bbox(c(xmin = xmin(annual_rate_of_change),
  ymin = ymin(annual_rate_of_change),
  xmax = xmax(annual_rate_of_change),
  ymax = ymax(annual_rate_of_change)),
  crs = st_crs(4326)) |>
st_as_sfc() |>
st_make_grid(cellsize = res(GBR_OISST_SSTA)[1], what = "polygons")
#grid_cells <- st_sf(id = 1:length(grid_cells), geometry = grid_cells)


# Create a polygon of non-NA cells in GBR_OISST_SSTA
mask <- mask(annual_rate_of_change, annual_rate_of_change >= 0.05)
mask[!is.na(mask)] <- 1

mask_points <- as.points(mask, na.rm = TRUE)
mask_sf <- st_as_sf(mask_points)
projected_crs <- "+proj=utm +zone=55 +south +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"
mask_projected <- st_transform(mask_sf, crs = projected_crs)
resolution_meters <- 25000  # 0.25 
mask_buffered <- mask_projected |>
  st_buffer(dist = resolution_meters/2, endCapStyle = "SQUARE")
mask_polygons <- st_transform(mask_buffered, crs = st_crs(mask_sf))


# Define the numeric breaks for the values and color scale
anom_numeric_breaks <- c(0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3)  # Numeric values to map colors
anom_colour_breaks <- c("#ffffff", "#f4a6a6", "#e03f3f", "#680202")  # Colors to use at these breaks

rate_change_plot <- ggplot() + 
  theme_bw() + 
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = annual_rate_of_change,  interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    limits = c(0.1, 0.3),
    name = "SST anom",
    na.value = "transparent",
    breaks = anom_numeric_breaks,       # Set custom breaks for the color scale
    labels = anom_numeric_breaks,       # Use the same values for labels
    colours = anom_colour_breaks,       # Apply colors directly
    values = scales::rescale(anom_numeric_breaks, from = c(0.1, 0.3))  
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 7, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = c(0.95, 0.95), 
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),  
    legend.text = element_text(size = 4, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 1.5)),  # Reduce margin around text
    legend.title = element_text(size = 5),  # Scale legend title
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "lines"),  # Scale legend box size
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA), 
    legend.margin = margin(2.1, 2.1, 2.1, 2.1)
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) + 
  geom_sf(data = ausmap, fill = "#E2E7D4", alpha = 1) +
  labs(title = "Decadal Rate of Change \nin SST anom (°C) 1982-2024", fill = "SST") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1))



###################################################################### 
# Maximum Degree Heating Week (1981-2025)
######################################################################


# Calculate the maximum DHW across all layers
max_DHW <- max(GBR_OISST_DHW, na.rm = TRUE)

# Define the numeric breaks for the values
dhw_numeric_breaks <- c(4, 8, 12, 16, 20)  # Numeric values to map colors
#dhw_colour_breaks <- c(, "#eddf8c", "#ff9a15", "#820000")  # Colors to use at these breaks
dhw_colour_breaks <- c( "#a5d3e5", "#eddf8c",  "#d47a04",  "#4d0101")  # Colors to use at these breaks



# Plot
max_DHW_plot <- ggplot() + 
  theme_bw() + 
  ggtitle("Max DHW 1982-2025") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = max_DHW, interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE) + 
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    limits = c(4, 20),
    name = "Max DHW",
    na.value = "transparent",
    breaks = dhw_numeric_breaks,       # Set custom breaks for the color scale
    labels = dhw_numeric_breaks,       # Use the same values for labels
    colours = dhw_colour_breaks,       # Apply colors directly
    values = scales::rescale(dhw_numeric_breaks, from = c(4, 20))  # Rescale numeric breaks to match color scale
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 7, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position = c(0.95, 0.95),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),  
    legend.text = element_text(size = 4, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 1.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 5),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA), 
    legend.margin = margin(2.1, 2.1, 2.1, 2.1)
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = ausmap, fill = "#E2E7D4", alpha = 1) +
  labs(title = "Maximum DHW \nexposure (1982-2025)", fill = "DHW") +
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1)  

###################################################################### 
# Warmest year (DHW)
######################################################################


# Extract the time information (days) and convert to years
time_info <- time(GBR_OISST_DHW)
years <- as.numeric(format(time_info, "%Y"))

# Aggregate daily data to annual maximum DHW
maxDHW_annual <- tapp(GBR_OISST_DHW, index = years, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)

df <- as.data.frame(maxDHW_annual, xy = TRUE)
colnames(df) <- gsub("^X", "", colnames(df))

df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(x,y), 
               names_to = "year", 
               values_to = "dhw") 

df_long_max <- df_long %>%
  group_by(x, y) %>%
  mutate(max_dhw = max(dhw, na.rm = TRUE),  # Find the max DHW for each group
         dhw = ifelse(dhw == max_dhw, dhw, NA),  # Set non-max dhw values to NA
         year = ifelse(!is.na(dhw), year, NA)) %>%  # Set non-max year values to NA
  dplyr::select(-max_dhw) |>   # Remove the temporary max_dhw column
  drop_na(year) |> 
  arrange(year)

df_wide_max <- df_long_max %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = dhw) %>%
  mutate(across(`1982`:`2025`, ~ as.factor(ifelse(!is.na(.), cur_column(), NA))))

year_max <- rast(df_wide_max, type = "xyz")

# Use the `app` function to merge the layers, keeping the first non-NA value
flattened_raster <- app(year_max, fun = function(x) {
  first_non_na <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(first_non_na) > 0) {
    return(first_non_na[1])  # Return the first non-NA value
  } else {
    return(0)  # If all values are NA, return NA
  }
})

values(flattened_raster) <- as.factor(values(flattened_raster))
flattened_raster <- droplevels(flattened_raster) # Drop NA factor levels
# Set the CRS 
crs(flattened_raster) <- "EPSG:4326"


library(RColorBrewer)
years <- as.character(1982:2025)
n_colors <- length(years)
spectral_palette <- colorRampPalette(c(rev(brewer.pal(6, "Greens")), rev(brewer.pal(8, "Blues")), (brewer.pal(8, "Reds"))))(n_colors)
year_colors <- setNames(spectral_palette, years)


###################################################################### 
# Frequency of Degree Heating Weeks exceeding 6DHW
######################################################################

dhw_year_max <- GBR_OISST_DHW %>%
  tapp(index = format(time(.), "%Y"), fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)


# Create hull polygon
hull <- data.frame(lat = c(-9, -25, -25), 
                   lon = c(136, 136, 151.5)) |> 
  sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326) |> 
  st_combine() |>            
  st_convex_hull() |>        
  st_cast("POLYGON") |> 
  st_make_valid()

# Create outline polygon and remove intersection with hull
ausoutlinefill <- ausmap |> 
  st_make_valid() |> 
  st_cast("MULTILINESTRING") |> 
  st_make_valid() |> 
  st_cast("POLYGON") |> 
  dplyr::select(scalerank) |>  
  st_difference(hull)  # Get parts outside the hull


ausoutline <- ausmap |>
  st_cast("MULTILINESTRING") |>
  dplyr::select(scalerank) |>
  st_crop(st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 142.05, ymin = -24.75, xmax = 154.00, ymax = -8.75), crs = 4326))) |>
  st_cast("LINESTRING") |>
  mutate(length_km = st_length(geometry) / 1000) |>
  arrange(desc(length_km))

ausoutline <- ausoutline[1,]

###################################################################### 
# Frequency of Degree Heating Weeks 
###################################################################### 

# 1986-1995
dhw_subset_1986_1995 <- subset(dhw_year_max, paste0("X", seq(1986, 1995, 1)))
dhw_subset_1986_1995 <- app(dhw_subset_1986_1995, fun = function(x) {
  sum(x > 6, na.rm = TRUE)})
dhw_subset_1986_1995 <- subst(dhw_subset_1986_1995, from = 0, to = NA)
values(dhw_subset_1986_1995) <- values(dhw_subset_1986_1995) |> as.factor()

# 1996-2005
dhw_subset_1996_2005 <- subset(dhw_year_max, paste0("X", seq(1996, 2005, 1)))
dhw_subset_1996_2005 <- app(dhw_subset_1996_2005, fun = function(x) {
  sum(x > 6, na.rm = TRUE)})
dhw_subset_1996_2005 <- subst(dhw_subset_1996_2005, from = 0, to = NA)
values(dhw_subset_1996_2005) <- values(dhw_subset_1996_2005) |> as.factor()

# 2006-2015
dhw_subset_2006_2015 <- subset(dhw_year_max, paste0("X", seq(2006, 2015, 1)))
dhw_subset_2006_2015 <- app(dhw_subset_2006_2015, fun = function(x) {
  sum(x > 6, na.rm = TRUE)})
dhw_subset_2006_2015 <- subst(dhw_subset_2006_2015, from = 0, to = NA)
values(dhw_subset_2006_2015) <- values(dhw_subset_2006_2015) |> as.factor()

# 2016-2025
dhw_subset_2016_2025 <- subset(dhw_year_max, paste0("X", seq(2016, 2025, 1)))
dhw_subset_2016_2025 <- app(dhw_subset_2016_2025, fun = function(x) {
  sum(x > 6, na.rm = TRUE)
})
dhw_subset_2016_2025 <- subst(dhw_subset_2016_2025, from = 0, to = NA)
values(dhw_subset_2016_2025) <- values(dhw_subset_2016_2025) |> as.factor()


# hack to set extent with lat
raster_df <- as.data.frame(annual_rate_of_change, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
current_extent <- ext(annual_rate_of_change)
longitude_range <- xmax(current_extent) - xmin(current_extent)
extended_xmax <- xmax(current_extent) +  longitude_range*1.5  # Extend 3x the longitude range

mask_polygons_border <- as.polygons(mask, dissolve = TRUE)
mask_sf <- st_as_sf(mask_polygons_border)
mask_border <- st_union(mask_sf)

# maxSST per year for OISST2 in rast
gbr_OISST2_dhw_annual_max <- dhw::summarise_raster(GBR_OISST2_DHW, index = "years", fun = max, na.rm = TRUE, overwrite=TRUE)

# hack to set extent with lat
raster_df <- as.data.frame(annual_rate_of_change, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
current_extent <- ext(annual_rate_of_change)
longitude_range <- xmax(current_extent) - xmin(current_extent)
extended_xmax <- xmax(current_extent) +  longitude_range  # Extend 3x the longitude range

mask_polygons_border <- as.polygons(mask, dissolve = TRUE)
mask_sf <- st_as_sf(mask_polygons_border)
mask_border <- st_union(mask_sf)


################## 1. rate change SSTA ########################
rate_change_plot <- ggplot() + 
  theme_bw() +
  
  # Y-axis with exactly 6 evenly spaced labels
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-25, -9),
                     breaks=seq(-25, -9, 2),
                     labels = paste0(seq(-25, -9, 2), "°N"))  +

  # X-axis with rotated labels
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(xmin(current_extent), extended_xmax + 2), 
    breaks = seq(140, 154, 2),
    labels = paste0(seq(140, 154, 2), "°E")
  ) +

  # Plot components
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = annual_rate_of_change, interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      frame.colour = "black",
      frame.linewidth = 0.2,
      ticks.colour = "black",
      ticks.linewidth = 0.2
    ),
    limits = c(0.175, 0.3),
    name = "SSTA",
    na.value = "transparent",
    breaks = anom_numeric_breaks,
    labels = anom_numeric_breaks,
    colours = anom_colour_breaks,
    values = scales::rescale(anom_numeric_breaks, from = c(0.1, 0.3))
  ) +

  # Theme settings
  theme(
    title = NULL,
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.position = c(0.28, 0.97),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 3.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA), 
    legend.margin = margin(3.1, 3.1, 3.1, 3.1),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")
  ) +

  # Overlay other geometries
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) + 
  geom_sf(data = mask_border, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = ausmap, fill = "#E2E7D4", alpha = 1) +
  
  # Label settings
  labs(fill = "SST")



################## 2. max DHW ########################

max_DHW_plot <- ggplot() + 
  theme_void() +
  
  # Spatial raster layer
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = max_DHW, interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE) +
  
  # Color scale for DHW
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      frame.colour = "black",
      frame.linewidth = 0.2,
      ticks.colour = "black",
      ticks.linewidth = 0.2
    ),
    limits = c(4, 20),
    name = bquote(DHW[max]),
    na.value = "transparent",
    breaks = dhw_numeric_breaks,
    labels = dhw_numeric_breaks,
    colours = dhw_colour_breaks,
    values = scales::rescale(dhw_numeric_breaks, from = c(4, 20))
  ) +

  # Theme and legend styling
  theme(
    title = NULL,
    axis.text.x = NULL,
    legend.position = c(0.5, 0.97),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 3.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(3.1, 3.1, 3.1, 3.1),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")
  ) +
  
  # Spatial data layers
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_border, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 1) +
  
  # Labels
  labs(fill = "DHW")



################## 3. max DHW ##################

max_dhw_year_plot <- ggplot() + 
  
  theme_void() +
  
  # Spatial raster layer
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = flattened_raster, interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE) +

  # Manual color scale for year
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = year_colors,
    na.value = "transparent",
    guide = guide_legend(na.translate = FALSE)
  ) +
  
  # Theme and legend styling
  theme(
    title = NULL,
    axis.text.x =NULL,
    legend.position = c(0.78, 0.98),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 3.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(3.1, 3.1, 3.1, 3.1),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")
  ) +
  
  # Legend layout (2 columns)
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 2)) +

  # Spatial data layers
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_border, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 1) +

  # Labels
  labs(title = "", fill = "Year")


################## 1. dhw_exceedance_1986_1995 ##################
dhw_exceedance_1986_1995 <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-25, -9),
                     breaks=seq(-25, -9, 2),
                     labels = paste0(seq(-25, -9, 2), "°N"))  +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(xmin(current_extent), extended_xmax + 4),
                     breaks=seq(140, 154, 2),
                     labels = paste0(seq(140, 154, 2), "°E"))  +
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = dhw_subset_1986_1995,  interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(11, "Spectral")))(7),
                    na.value = "transparent"
                    #guide = guide_legend(na.translate = FALSE),
                  ) +
  theme(
    title = NULL,
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.position = c(0.75, 0.98),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 3.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.75, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(3.1, 3.1, 3.1, 3.1),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")  # Add black border around legend keys
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_border, fill = NA, linewidth=0.5, alpha = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = ausmap, fill = "#E2E7D4", alpha = 1) +
  labs(fill = "n")

################## 2. dhw_exceedance_1995_2004 ##################

dhw_exceedance_1995_2004 <- ggplot() +
  theme_void() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-25, -9),
                     breaks=seq(-25, -9, 2),
                     labels = paste0(seq(-25, -9, 2), "°N"))  +

  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = dhw_subset_1996_2005,  interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(11, "Spectral")))(7),
                    na.value = "transparent"
                    #guide = guide_legend(na.translate = FALSE),
                  ) +
  theme(
    title = NULL,
    axis.text.x = NULL,
    legend.position = c(0.56, 0.98),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 3.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.75, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(3.1, 3.1, 3.1, 3.1),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")  # Add black border around legend keys
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_border, fill = NA, linewidth=0.5, alpha = 1) +
 # geom_sf(data = ausmap, fill = "#E2E7D4", alpha = 1) +
  labs(fill = "n")


################## 3. dhw_exceedance_2005_2014 ##################

dhw_exceedance_2005_2014 <- ggplot() +
  theme_void() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-25, -9),
                     breaks=seq(-25, -9, 2),
                     labels = paste0(seq(-25, -9, 2), "°N"))  +

  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = dhw_subset_2006_2015,  interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(11, "Spectral")))(7),
                    na.value = "transparent"
                    #guide = guide_legend(na.translate = FALSE),
                  ) +
  theme(
    title = NULL,
    axis.text.x = NULL,
    legend.position = c(0.75, 0.98),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 3.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.75, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(3.1, 3.1, 3.1, 3.1),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")  # Add black border around legend keys
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_border, fill = NA, linewidth=0.5, alpha = 1) +
  #geom_sf(data = ausmap, fill = "#E2E7D4", alpha = 1) +
  labs(fill = "n")

dhw_subset_2016_2025_2 <- dhw_subset_2016_2025
dhw_subset_2016_2025_2 <- classify(dhw_subset_2016_2025_2, cbind(NA, 0))
dhw_subset_2016_2025_2 <- as.factor(dhw_subset_2016_2025_2)


################## 4. dhw_exceedance_2015_2024 ##################

dhw_exceedance_2015_2024 <- ggplot() +
  theme_void() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-25, -9),
                     breaks=seq(-25, -9, 2),
                     labels = paste0(seq(-25, -9, 2), "°N"))  +
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = dhw_subset_2016_2025_2,  interpolate = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE) +
   scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("transparent", colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(11, "Spectral")))(7)),  # Adjust color for 0-7 (8 levels)
    breaks = 0:7,                           # Order legend from 0 to 7
    labels = as.character(0:7),             # Labels from 0 to 7
    na.value = "transparent",               # Ensure NA is not visualized
    name = "> 6DHW events \n per decade"                       # Legend title
  ) +
  theme(
    title = NULL,
    axis.text.x = NULL,
    legend.position = c(0.85, 0.98),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 1, b = 0, l = 3.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 6.5, hjust=0.2),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(3.1, 3.1, 3.1, 3.1),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")  # Add black border around legend keys
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_polygons, fill = NA, linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = mask_border, fill = NA, linewidth=0.5, alpha = 1) +
  #geom_sf(data = ausmap, fill = "#E2E7D4", alpha = 1) +
  labs(fill = "N events")



######## COMBINED
library(patchwork)

titlesize <- theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8))

### A
mainfig1 <-wrap_elements(main_dhw +
  geom_text(data=df.labels, aes(date, dhw, label=label), size=2.5, angle = 90) +
  inset_element(inset, left = 0.02, bottom = 0.3, right = 0.58, top = 0.98) ) 

### B
dhw_maps <- rate_change_plot + ggtitle(expression(Warming~"("*degree*C~yr^{-1}*")")) + titlesize +
  inset_element(max_DHW_plot, left = 0.03, bottom = 0, right = 1.03, top = 1.07) + ggtitle(expression(DHW[max])) + titlesize +
  inset_element(max_dhw_year_plot, left = 0.27, bottom = 0, right = 1.27, top = 1.07) + ggtitle(expression(Year~of~DHW[max])) + titlesize

### C
dhw_6dhw_maps <- dhw_exceedance_1986_1995 + ggtitle("1986-1995") + titlesize +
  inset_element(dhw_exceedance_1995_2004 + ggtitle("1996-2005"), left = -0.09, bottom = 0, right = 1-0.09, top = 1.06) + titlesize +
  inset_element(dhw_exceedance_2005_2014 + ggtitle("2006-2015"), left = 0.1, bottom = 0, right = 1.1, top = 1.06) + titlesize +
  inset_element(dhw_exceedance_2015_2024 + ggtitle("2016-2025"), left = 0.28, bottom = 0, right = 1.28, top = 1.06) + titlesize 

combined_maps <- (wrap_elements(dhw_maps) | wrap_elements(dhw_6dhw_maps))  +  
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.15))


figure1 <- mainfig1 / combined_maps  +  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1)) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") 

figure1

#ggsave(figure1, filename="~/Desktop/figure1.pdf", dpi="retina", width=15, height=10, units="in", scale=1)


```

### Spatial patterns of thermal stress drive mass coral bleaching events

The bleaching response has intensified across the last two decades in step with the thermal forcing. The earliest events, in 1998 and 2002, were spatially patchy and of generally low-to-moderate severity (Figure 2a). From 2016 onward, whole regions began bleaching to very high levels, in the 2016, 2017 and 2020 events. This intensified further in 2022 and 2024, which were the first events to register the highest "extreme" bleaching category (Figure 2a). The severity of recent events is unprecedented in the 85-year reconstructed record and consistent with the highest sea-surface temperatures in four centuries (Henley et al 2024).

The total bleaching impact recorded at each event expressed as both the number of reefs and the reef area per category rises steeply across the record (Fig. 2b). In 1998 and 2002 most surveyed reef area sat in the no-to-low classes, whereas the recent events are dominated by the high and very-high categories, and the extreme class appears only in the most recent events (Figure 2b). Aerial surveys indicate a clear expanding bleaching footprint, with bleaching affecting 60–90% of reef area in the most recent events (2016–2025).

Because survey extent itself varies among years, the panel reports both counts and area; on either measure the distribution shifts unambiguously toward more severe bleaching through time. As the GBR is a complex system of reefs across 2000km, we used a Bayesian spatial proportional-odds model to explore how the DHW and spatial structure of reefs drive the bleaching response across the eight events.

### Intensification of mass coral bleaching on the GBR

Modelling the response of bleaching to DHW confirms that DHW raises the probability of a reef exceeding higher bleaching categories (Figure 2c). While this was expected given the established experimental and observational data across the past four decades (references), the year effects show diverging trends across events. After accounting for thermal stress and for reef location (the spatial field and reef-level term), the year intercepts rise sharply in recent events. A large positive effect in 2016 relative to the 1998 baseline indicates a bleaching susceptibility that DHW alone cannot explain, and the high positive effects in 2022, 2024 and 2025 point to a new baseline regime in which reefs bleach more severely for a given level of heat. Critically, the DHW × year interactions are small and mixed in sign, indicating that the sensitivity of bleaching to each additional degree-week is broadly conserved across events. The mechanistic thermal-threshold response therefore appears stable, but the physiological and ecological state (i.e. energy reserves, recovery state, community composition, chronic stress) shifts the whole system with each event. 

The fitted cumulative-probability curves across events show how the response to thermal dose itself has moved (Fig. 2d). For each event, the probability of occupying each ordered bleaching class is plotted across the observed DHW gradient, truncated at each event's maximum surveyed DHW. The severe band expands progressively leftward through the record while the no-bleaching band contracts even at low thermal stress, so that very high bleaching now occurs at a markedly lower dose than in the earliest events. These results suggest that severe bleaching has become more likely at a given thermal dose not because reefs respond more steeply to each additional degree-week, but because the entire dose–response curve has shifted, mapping every level of heat to a higher bleaching probability than in earlier events. At equivalent thermal stress, reefs have become progressively more likely to bleach,  representing a shift in contextual vulnerability rather than in the thermal dose (dhw~bleaching) itself. 

Holding thermal dose fixed isolates this shift most directly (Fig. 2e). At a constant 8 °C-weeks of DHW, the model-estimated probability of a reef equalling or exceeding the higher bleaching classes rises steeply across the record — the probability of exceeding the very-high class increasing from roughly 0.15 in 1998 to around 0.7 in 2024. In simple terms, for an identical level of heat stress, severe bleaching has become several times more likely over the study period, indicating increasing vulnerability.

Several non-exclusive mechanisms could raise bleaching probability at fixed DHW. i) DHW does not fully capture marine-heatwave character, and recent events may carry longer duration, higher night-time minima or earlier onset than the metric reflects, ii) cumulative legacy effects are also plausible: repeated bleaching erodes coral condition, shortens recovery windows and can shift communities toward more stress-sensitive states, iii) Co-stressors — light and water clarity, low wind driving high irradiance, runoff and turbidity, or disease may further modulate the response in either direction. iv) OISST isn't the best proxy, etc etc (but see Figure Sx)


```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-height: 9
#| fig-width: 12

##### interpolate aerial scores


library(dhw)
library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
library(purrr)
library(lubridate)
library(sf)
library(patchwork)
library(stars)
library(gstat)
library(concaveman)
library(units)


gbr_reef_subset <- gbr_reef_outline |>
  st_zm() |>
  filter(!FEAT_NAME %in% c("Mainland", "Other")) |>
  filter(X_COORD > 140) |>
  filter(Y_COORD > -24.3) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_transform(4326)

GBRMPpolygon <- gbr_reef_subset |>
  concaveman(concavity = 2, length_threshold = 0) |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_buffer(1000)


# make inverse mask
inverse_GBRMPpolygon <- st_difference(
  GBRMPpolygon |> st_set_crs(4326) |>
    st_transform(32755) |> st_buffer(25000),
  GBRMPpolygon |> st_set_crs(4326) |>
    st_transform(32755)
) |> st_transform(4326)


# load aerial data with DHW
gbr_shape_aerial_OISST <- readRDS("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/gbr_shape_aerial_OISST.rds") |>
  mutate(aerial_score = max_aerial_score) |>
  select(-median_aerial_score, -max_aerial_score)

gbr_reef_subset <- gbr_reef_outline |>
  st_zm() |>
  filter(!FEAT_NAME %in% c("Mainland", "Other")) |>
  filter(X_COORD > 140) |>
  filter(Y_COORD > -24.3) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_transform(4326)

gbr_reef_subset_points <- gbr_reef_subset |> st_centroid()

GBRMPpolygon <- gbr_reef_subset |>
  concaveman(concavity = 2, length_threshold = 0) |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_buffer(1000)

GBRMPpolygon98_south <- gbr_reef_subset |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_crop(st_bbox(c(xmin = 140, ymin = -24, xmax = 155, ymax = -15))) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  concaveman::concaveman() |> #  st_zm() |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_buffer(10000) |>
  st_make_valid()

GBRMPpolygon98_north <- gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  filter(year %in% c(1998, 2002)) |>
  group_by(LABEL_ID) %>%
  # summarise(geometry = st_union(geometry)) |>
  st_cast("POLYGON") |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  ungroup() |>
  concaveman::concaveman() |>
  st_zm() |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_buffer(10000) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_crop(st_bbox(c(xmin = 142, ymin = -15, xmax = 155, ymax = -10)))

GBRMPpolygon1998_2002 <- st_union(GBRMPpolygon98_north, GBRMPpolygon98_south)

GBRMPpolygon2025 <- gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  filter(year %in% 2025) |>
  group_by(LABEL_ID) %>%
  # summarise(geometry = st_union(geometry)) |>
  st_cast("POLYGON") |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  ungroup() |>
  concaveman::concaveman() |>
  st_zm() |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_buffer(10000) |>
  st_make_valid()

ausmap_outline <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "large", country = "Australia") |>
  st_transform(st_crs(4283))

ausmap <- ausmap_outline %>%
  st_crop(., st_bbox(c(xmin = 141.614956, ymin = -24.607859, xmax = 152.139185, ymax = -10.9)))


# create IDW spatial interpolations:
grd <- st_bbox(gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |> st_transform(32755)) |>
  st_as_stars(dx = 5000) |>
  st_crop(GBRMPpolygon |> st_transform(32755))

grd_1998_2002 <- st_bbox(gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |> st_transform(32755)) |>
  st_as_stars(dx = 5000) |>
  st_crop(GBRMPpolygon1998_2002 |> st_transform(32755))

grd_2025 <- st_bbox(gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |> st_transform(32755)) |>
  st_as_stars(dx = 5000) |>
  st_crop(GBRMPpolygon2025 |> st_transform(32755))

library(gstat)
heatmap_1998 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 1998), grd_1998_2002,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x1998", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x1998") %>%
  suppressMessages()

heatmap_2002 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 2002), grd_1998_2002,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x2002", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x2002") %>%
  suppressMessages()

heatmap_2016 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 2016), grd,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x2016", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x2016") %>%
  suppressMessages()

heatmap_2017 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 2017), grd,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x2017", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x2017") %>%
  suppressMessages()

heatmap_2020 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 2020), grd,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x2020", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x2020") %>%
  suppressMessages()

heatmap_2022 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 2022), grd,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x2022", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x2022") %>%
  suppressMessages()

heatmap_2024 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 2024), grd,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x2024", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x2024") %>%
  suppressMessages()

heatmap_2025 <- gstat::idw(aerial_score ~ 1, gbr_shape_aerial_OISST |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  tidyterra::filter(year == 2025), grd_2025,
idp = 4, nmax = 200, debug.level = 0
) %>%
  `names<-`(c("x2025", names(.)[2])) %>%
  dplyr::select("x2025") %>%
  suppressMessages()


# resample 1998, 2002, 2025 to match the common grid in stars with st_warp
heatmap_1998_resampled <- st_warp(heatmap_1998, st_as_stars(heatmap_2016))
heatmap_2002_resampled <- st_warp(heatmap_2002, st_as_stars(heatmap_2016))
heatmap_2025_resampled <- st_warp(heatmap_2025, st_as_stars(heatmap_2024))

# Merge all resampled rasters
maps <- c(
  heatmap_1998_resampled, heatmap_2002_resampled,
  heatmap_2016, heatmap_2017, heatmap_2020,
  heatmap_2022, heatmap_2024, heatmap_2025_resampled
) |> terra::rast()

names(maps) <- c("1998", "2002", "2016", "2017", "2020", "2022", "2024", "2025")

sum_raster <- app(maps, fun = sum)

# pal <- c(
# 
#   "#313695",
#   "#4575B4",
#   "#74ADD1",
#   "#ABD9E9",
#   "#E0F3F8",
#   "#FFFFBF",
#   "#FEE090",
#   "#FDAE61",
#   "#F46D43",
#   "#D73027",
#   "#D73027"
# 
# )


ausmap_plot <- ausmap |> st_transform(4326)
maps_plot <- terra::project(maps, "EPSG:4326")
maps_projected <- project(maps, "EPSG:4326") |>
  mask(GBRMPpolygon)

# make inverse mask
inverse_GBRMPpolygon <- st_difference(
  GBRMPpolygon |> st_set_crs(4326) |>
    st_transform(32755) |> st_buffer(25000),
  GBRMPpolygon |> st_set_crs(4326) |>
    st_transform(32755)
) |> st_transform(4326)


#aerial_colors_old <- c("#6ca8c8", "#73b15c", "#fafa6e", "#d07b61", "#bd3e42", "#581845")

aerial_colors_six <- c("#ABD9E9", "#FFFFBF", "#FEE090", "#FDAE61", "#F46D43",  "#D73027")

scale_fill_aerial <- scale_fill_gradientn(
  colors = aerial_colors_six,
  name = "",
  na.value = "transparent",
  limits = c(-0.1, 5.1),
  breaks = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5), # Breaks corresponding to the labels
  labels = c(
    "None (<1%)",
    "Low (1-10%)",
    "Medium (11-30%)",
    "High (31-60%)",
    "Very high (61-90%)",
    "Extreme (>91%)"
  ) # New categorical labels
)



maps_crop_1998 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[1]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf() |> st_make_valid()
maps_crop_2002 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[2]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf() 
maps_crop_2016 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[3]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
maps_crop_2017 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[4]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
maps_crop_2020 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[5]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
maps_crop_2022 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[6]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
maps_crop_2024 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[7]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
maps_crop_2025 <- crop(as.polygons(maps_projected[[8]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()


aerial_map_1998 <- ggplot() +
#  theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_1998, aes(fill = `1998`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  geom_sf(data = inverse_GBRMPpolygon |> st_transform(4326), fill = "white", linewidth = 0.0) +
  geom_sf(data = ausmap |> st_transform(4326), fill = "olivedrab4", alpha = 0.2, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  # geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon1998_2002, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + 
  ggtitle("1998")

aerial_map_2002 <- ggplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_2002, aes(fill = `2002`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon1998_2002, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +
  ggtitle("2002")

aerial_map_2016 <- ggplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_2016, aes(fill = `2016`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +
  ggtitle("2016")

aerial_map_2017 <- ggplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_2017, aes(fill = `2017`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +
  ggtitle("2017")

aerial_map_2020 <- ggplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_2020, aes(fill = `2020`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +
  ggtitle("2020")

aerial_map_2022 <- ggplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_2022, aes(fill = `2022`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +
  ggtitle("2022")

aerial_map_2024 <- ggplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_2024, aes(fill = `2024`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +
  ggtitle("2024")

aerial_map_2025 <- ggplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, fill = "white") +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data = maps_crop_2025, aes(fill = `2025`), linewidth = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon2025, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  geom_sf(data = GBRMPpolygon, linewidth = 0.25, color = "black", fill = NA) +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +
  ggtitle("2025")

legend_only <- ggplot() +
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = maps_projected[[8]], show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_aerial +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.3, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Aerial Bleaching Score"))

library(cowplot)
library(grid)
legend_only <- cowplot::get_legend(legend_only) |> cowplot::plot_grid()


```



```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-height: 9
#| fig-width: 12

##### interpolate spatial DHW


library(terra)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyterra)
library(sf)
library(ggtext)
library(concaveman)


# Load SpatRaster

GBR_OISST_DHW_annual <- dhw::summarise_raster(GBR_OISST_DHW, index = "years", fun = max, na.rm = TRUE, overwrite=TRUE) |> 
  crop(vect(GBRMPpolygon |> st_transform(st_crs(GBR_OISST_DHW))))

# Define static elements
current_extent <- ext(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual)
longitude_range <- xmax(current_extent) - xmin(current_extent)
extended_xmax <- xmax(current_extent) + longitude_range * 3

dhw_scale <-  c("#006f99", "#00A6E5", "#FFD700", "#FF8C00", "#B20000", "#660000", "#3D0000")
scale_fill_bleaching <- scale_fill_gradientn(
  limits = c(0, 21),
  name = expression(DHW[max]),
  na.value = "transparent",
  breaks = c(0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21),
  labels = c(0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21),
  colours = dhw_scale,
  values = scales::rescale(c(0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21), from = c(0, 20))
)


gbr_dhw_vect_1998 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_1998"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf() |> st_make_valid()  
gbr_dhw_vect_2002 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_2002"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf() 
gbr_dhw_vect_2016 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_2016"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
gbr_dhw_vect_2017 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_2017"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
gbr_dhw_vect_2020 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_2020"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
gbr_dhw_vect_2022 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_2022"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
gbr_dhw_vect_2024 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_2024"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()
gbr_dhw_vect_2025 <- crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_2025"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) #|> st_as_sf()

#tm_shape(crop(as.polygons(GBR_OISST_DHW_annual[["y_1998"]]), vect(GBRMPpolygon)) ) + tm_polygons("y_1998", lwd=0)

gbr_dhw_vect_1998_plot <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_1998, aes(fill=y_1998), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  geom_sf(data = inverse_GBRMPpolygon |> st_transform(4326), fill="white", linewidth=0.0) +
  geom_sf(data=ausmap |> st_transform(4326), fill="olivedrab4", alpha=0.2, color="black", linewidth=0.2) +
  #geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon1998_2002, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
   theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
    ) +ggtitle("1998")

gbr_dhw_vect_2002_plot <- ggplot() + theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_2002, aes(fill=y_2002), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2002")

gbr_dhw_vect_2016_plot <- ggplot() + theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_2016, aes(fill=y_2016), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2016")

gbr_dhw_vect_2017_plot <- ggplot() + theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_2017, aes(fill=y_2017), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2017")

gbr_dhw_vect_2020_plot <- ggplot() + theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_2020, aes(fill=y_2020), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2020")

gbr_dhw_vect_2022_plot <- ggplot() + theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_2022, aes(fill=y_2022), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2022")

gbr_dhw_vect_2024_plot <- ggplot() + theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_2024, aes(fill=y_2024), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2024")

gbr_dhw_vect_2025_plot <- ggplot() + theme_minimal() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatvector(data=gbr_dhw_vect_2025, aes(fill=y_2025), linewidth=0, show.legend=FALSE) + 
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  #geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon2025, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2025")

legend_dhw_only <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill="lightgrey", fill=NA) +
  tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = maps_projected[[8]], show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_bleaching +
  theme_void() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  )  +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "DHWmax"))

library(cowplot)
library(grid)
legend_dhw_only <- cowplot::get_legend(legend_dhw_only) |> cowplot::plot_grid()

plotmin=-0.35
dhw_plot_panel <- ggdraw(xlim = c(0, 1.28), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_1998_plot, plotmin, 0, 1, 1) +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2002_plot, plotmin+0.125, 0, 1, 1) +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2016_plot, plotmin+0.25, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2017_plot, plotmin+0.38, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2020_plot, plotmin+0.505, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2022_plot, plotmin+0.625, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2024_plot, plotmin+0.745, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2025_plot, plotmin+0.865, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(legend_dhw_only, plotmin+0.92, 0.25, 1, 1)


plotmin <- 0
plot_width <- 0.28  # Slightly larger than spacing
plot_height <- 1


dhw_plot_panel <- ggdraw(xlim = c(0, 1.35), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_1998_plot, plotmin, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2002_plot, plotmin + 0.125, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2016_plot, plotmin + 0.25, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2017_plot, plotmin + 0.38, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2020_plot, plotmin + 0.505, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2022_plot, plotmin + 0.625, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2024_plot, plotmin + 0.745, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(gbr_dhw_vect_2025_plot, plotmin + 0.865, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(legend_dhw_only, plotmin + 0.64, 0.2, 1, 1)



aerial_plot_panel <- ggdraw(xlim = c(0, 1.35), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_1998, plotmin, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_2002, plotmin + 0.125, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_2016, plotmin + 0.25, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_2017, plotmin + 0.38, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_2020, plotmin + 0.505, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_2022, plotmin + 0.625, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_2024, plotmin + 0.745, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(aerial_map_2025, plotmin + 0.865, 0, plot_width, plot_height) +
    draw_plot(legend_only, plotmin + 0.64, 0.2, 1, 1)



gbr_reef_area <- gbr_reef_subset %>% mutate(area = st_area(.))


### plot years

all_events_year <- gbr_shape_aerial_OISST %>%
  st_make_valid() |>
  mutate(area = st_area(.data$geometry)) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  group_by(year, aerial_score) |>
  summarise(count = n(), area = sum(area), .groups = "drop") |>
  # select(-geometry) |>
  arrange(year, desc(aerial_score)) |> # Reverse order before plotting
  mutate(aerial_score = factor(aerial_score, levels = rev(c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4", "5")))) |> 
  mutate(prop = (area / sum(gbr_reef_area$area)) * 100) |> 
  mutate(area = as.numeric(area))

all_events_proportion = all_events_year |>
  filter(aerial_score %in% c(1,2,3,4)) |> 
  group_by(year) |> 
  summarise(prop = sum(prop))
# 
# aerial_year_area <- ggplot() +
#   theme_bw() +
#   geom_bar(
#     data = all_events_year, aes(x = factor(year), y = area, fill = aerial_score),
#     stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", show.legend = FALSE
#   ) +
#   scale_fill_manual(
#     name = "", # Removes the default legend title
#     values = c(
#       "5" = "#5A0E0E", # Dark red → slightly muted
#       "4" = "#D53B50", # Red → softer
#       "3" = "#FF784E", # Orange-red → softened
#       "2" = "#FFFF80", # Yellow → less harsh
#       "1" = "#5BC36E", # Green → softer tone
#       "0" = "#4DADE6"
#     ), # Blue → slightly lighter
#     labels = c(
#       "No bleaching (<1%)",
#       "Low bleaching (1-10%)",
#       "Medium bleaching (11-30%)",
#       "High bleaching (31-60%)",
#       "Very high bleaching (61-90%)",
#       "Extreme bleaching (>91%)"
#     ) # Updated categorical labels
#   ) +
#   labs(x = "Year", y = expression(italic(n) ~ "reefs")) + # Italicize "n" in y-label
#   theme(legend.position = c(0.1, 0.85)) +
#   guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) # Reverse legend order to match

library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

area_scale <- max(all_events_year$count, na.rm = TRUE) /
  max(all_events_year$area / 1e6, na.rm = TRUE)

aerial_year_area <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_col(
    data = all_events_year,
    aes(
      x = factor(year),
      y = count,
      fill = aerial_score
    ),
    position = "stack",
    color = "black",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  # geom_point(
  #   data = all_events_year |>
  #     group_by(year) |>
  #     summarise(area_km2 = sum(area, na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6, .groups = "drop"),
  #   aes(
  #     x = factor(year),
  #     y = area_km2 * area_scale
  #   ),
  #   size = 2
  # ) +
  # geom_line(
  #   data = all_events_year |>
  #     group_by(year) |>
  #     summarise(area_km2 = sum(area, na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6, .groups = "drop"),
  #   aes(
  #     x = factor(year),
  #     y = area_km2 * area_scale,
  #     group = 1
  #   ),
  #   linewidth = 0.6
  # ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = expression(italic(n) ~ "reefs"),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      trans = ~ . / area_scale,
      name = expression("Reef area surveyed" ~ (km^2))
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = c(
      "5" = "#D73027",
      "4" = "#F46D43",
      "3" = "#FDAE61",
      "2" = "#FEE090",
      "1" = "#FFFFBF",
      "0" = "#ABD9E9"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "No bleaching (<1%)",
      "Low bleaching (1-10%)",
      "Medium bleaching (11-30%)",
      "High bleaching (31-60%)",
      "Very high bleaching (61-90%)",
      "Extreme bleaching (>91%)"
    )
  ) +
  labs(x = "Year") +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.1, 0.85)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE))


```


```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: false
#| fig-height: 9
#| fig-width: 12

#### run INLA for two spatial fields: polygons (broad spatial patterns) and mesh




library(sf)
library(tidyverse)
library(INLA)
library(concaveman)
library(fmesher)
library(terra)
library(kableExtra)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggridges)


#The spatial field is a regional-scale gradient (~350 km range, replicated across two pcmatern #fits and close to the matern's 440 km), of substantial amplitude (SD ~3 logits), and robust #to the PC range prior. The reef IID variance is negligible once the field and fixed effects #are included.
```
