And the year-level fixed effects — both the contextual-vulnerability intercepts #and the dhw:year slopes — are insensitive to the spatial prior specification (differences in #the 4th decimal). 

```{r}
## load observation data

dhw_inla <- readRDS("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/gbr_shape_aerial_OISST.rds") |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  mutate(aerial = case_when(
    max_aerial_score == 0 ~ "0",
    max_aerial_score == 1 ~ "1",
    max_aerial_score == 2 ~ "2",
    max_aerial_score == 3 ~ "3",
    max_aerial_score == 4 ~ "4",
    max_aerial_score == 5~ "4",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ) ) |>
  mutate(aerial = as.numeric(as.character(aerial)) + 1) |>  # note - +1 val
  select(-max_aerial_score, -median_aerial_score)

# load boundary
# GBRMPpolygon_complex <- st_read("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/archive/datasets/aerialshp/TS_GBRMP_FEATURES.shp", quiet=TRUE) |>

gbr_reef_outline <- readRDS("~/GBR-dhw/datasets/gbr_reef_outline.rds")


GBRMPpolygon_complex <- gbr_reef_outline |>
  dplyr::filter(FEAT_NAME=="Reef") |>
  concaveman::concaveman(concavity = 2, length_threshold = 0) |>
  st_zm() |>
  st_transform(32755) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_buffer(10000)

GBRMPpolygon <- GBRMPpolygon_complex |> st_simplify(dTolerance = 10000, preserveTopology = FALSE)

# make prediction grid
GBRMPpredict_grid <- st_make_grid(st_bbox(GBRMPpolygon), cellsize = 27830, square = FALSE) |>
  st_as_sf() |>
  st_filter(GBRMPpolygon, .predicate = st_intersects)

OISST_dhw <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST_DHW.tif")
OISST_dhw <- project(OISST_dhw, "EPSG:32755")
OISST_annual_max_dhw <- terra::tapp(OISST_dhw, index = "years", fun = max, na.rm = TRUE, overwrite=TRUE)
OISST_annual_max_dhw <- OISST_annual_max_dhw[[paste0("y_",  c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025))]]
names(OISST_annual_max_dhw) <- sub("^y_", "", names(OISST_annual_max_dhw))

# update with processed OISST
GBRMP_aerial <- dhw_inla  |> arrange(year, aerial) |> mutate(year=factor(year, levels = c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025)))
GBRMP_aerial_centroids <- GBRMP_aerial |> st_centroid()

# extract DHW for the prediction grid and convert to points
GBRMPpredict_grid_dhw <- list()
for (i in 1:8) {
  #for (i in 1:length(names(OISST_annual_max_dhw))) {
  GBRMPpredict_grid$dhw <- exactextractr::exact_extract(OISST_annual_max_dhw[[i]], GBRMPpredict_grid, fun = "max", progress = FALSE, weights = "area")
  GBRMPpredict_grid_dhw[[i]] <- GBRMPpredict_grid |> mutate(year = as.factor(names(OISST_annual_max_dhw)[[i]]))
}
GBRMPpredict_grid_dhw <- do.call(rbind, GBRMPpredict_grid_dhw)
GBR_grid <- GBRMPpredict_grid_dhw |> na.omit()
GBR_grid_centroids <- GBRMPpredict_grid_dhw |> st_centroid() |> na.omit()

# make points along linestring for border extent
boundary <- GBR_grid_centroids |>
  select(-year, -dhw) |>
  distinct() |>
  concaveman::concaveman() |>
  st_buffer(10000) |>
  st_boundary()

# get boundary points and make mesh
pts <- GBRMPpolygon |> st_coordinates() |> as.data.frame() |> select(1,2) |> as.matrix()

# max.edge is the largest allowed triangle length; the lower the number the higher the resolution max.edge = c(inside the boundary triangle, outside the boundary triangle).
# offset is defining how far you want to extend your domain (i.e. a secondary boundary box) for inner boundary and outer boundary; the offset goes with the max edge
# see https://sites.stat.washington.edu/peter/591/Lindgren.pdf

mesh <- fmesher::fm_mesh_2d_inla(loc.domain = pts[,1:2], boundary = GBRMPpolygon, max.edge = c(40000, 800000), offset = c(5000, 10000), crs="EPSG:32755")

# Define the SPDE model using the mesh
#spde <- inla.spde2.matern(mesh = mesh, alpha = 2)

spde <- inla.spde2.pcmatern(
  mesh,
  alpha = 2,
  prior.range = c(150000, 0.5),   # P(range < 150 km) = 0.5   -- metres (EPSG:32755)
  prior.sigma = c(3, 0.01))       # P(sigma > 3) = 0.01  -- field SD ~3.7 was observed, so allow it

# Create a projection matrix for the observed data
A_obs <- inla.spde.make.A(mesh = mesh, loc = st_coordinates(GBRMP_aerial_centroids))

# Create an index for the spatial field (random effect) in the SPDE model
s_index <- inla.spde.make.index(name = "spatial.field", n.spde = spde$n.spde)

# Prepare the data stack for the observed  data
obs_stack <- inla.stack(
  data = list(aerial = GBRMP_aerial_centroids$aerial),  # Response variable
  A = list(A_obs, 1),  # Projection matrix for the spatial model and fixed effects
  effects = list(
    c(s_index, list(Intercept = 1)),  # Spatial random effects + intercept
    list(dhw = GBRMP_aerial_centroids$dhw, year = GBRMP_aerial_centroids$year, LABEL_ID = GBRMP_aerial_centroids$LABEL_ID)  # Covariates: dhw and year
  ),
  tag = "obs"  # Tag for the observed data stack
)


# repeat expand_grid for each grid-cell layout
min_dhw <- 0  # Minimum DHW
max_dhw <- 16
dhw_seq <- seq(min_dhw, max_dhw, length.out = 17)  # Generate 100 values between min and max DHW
unique_years <- unique(GBR_grid_centroids$year)
pred_grid <- expand.grid(dhw = dhw_seq, year = unique_years) # Create a grid of dhw and year combinations
n_locs <- nrow(GBR_grid_centroids) # Number of spatial locations
expanded_coords <- st_coordinates(GBR_grid_centroids)[rep(1:n_locs, times = nrow(pred_grid)), ] # Replicate the spatial locations for each dhw-year combination

# make preds mesh / stack
A_pred_expanded <- inla.spde.make.A(mesh = mesh, loc = expanded_coords)
expanded_grid <- pred_grid[rep(1:nrow(pred_grid), each = n_locs), ]
expanded_grid_sf <- cbind(expanded_grid, expanded_coords) |> st_as_sf(coords=c("X", "Y"), crs=32755)

preds_stack <- inla.stack(
  data = list(aerial = NA),  # NA for predictions
  A = list(A_pred_expanded, 1),
  effects = list(
    c(list(Intercept = 1)),  # Spatial random effects + intercept for prediction
    list(dhw = expanded_grid$dhw, year = expanded_grid$year, LABEL_ID = NA)  # Expanded dhw and year
  ),
  tag = "preds"
)

# polygon prediction stack

gbr_reef_32755 <- gbr_reef_subset |>          # <- the reef POLYGON layer
  st_make_valid() |> st_transform(32755)

yrs <- names(OISST_annual_max_dhw)            # "1998","2002",...,"2025"

reef_year_dhw <- lapply(seq_along(yrs), function(i) {
  dhw_i <- exactextractr::exact_extract(
    OISST_annual_max_dhw[[i]],               # year i's DHW raster layer
    gbr_reef_32755,                          # extract onto each reef polygon
    fun = "max", progress = FALSE)
  gbr_reef_32755 |>
    transmute(TARGET_FID, LABEL_ID, year = yrs[i], dhw = dhw_i)
}) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  filter(is.finite(dhw))
pred_pts <- reef_year_dhw |>
  st_centroid()

year_levels <- union(levels(GBRMP_aerial_centroids$year), sort(unique(pred_pts$year)))
id_levels   <- union(levels(factor(GBRMP_aerial_centroids$LABEL_ID)), sort(unique(pred_pts$LABEL_ID)))

pred_pts2 <- pred_pts |>
  mutate(
    year = factor(as.character(year), levels = year_levels),
    LABEL_ID = factor(as.character(LABEL_ID), levels = id_levels)
  )

# build reef_year_dhw + pred_pts2 with harmonised factor levels as before, then:
pred_stack_poly <- inla.stack(
  data = list(aerial = NA),
  A = list(inla.spde.make.A(mesh, st_coordinates(pred_pts2)), 1),
  effects = list(c(s_index, list(Intercept = 1)),
                 list(dhw = pred_pts2$dhw, year = pred_pts2$year, 
                      LABEL_ID = pred_pts2$LABEL_ID)),
  tag = "pred_poly")

combined_stack <- inla.stack(obs_stack, preds_stack, pred_stack_poly)   


# Define the INLA formula (full spatial model)
inla_formula <- aerial ~ dhw * year + f(spatial.field, model = spde) + f(LABEL_ID, model = "iid")

# Define fixed effect priors
fixedpriors <- list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE, config = TRUE, return.marginals.predictor = TRUE)

fm1 <- inla(
  inla_formula,
  data = inla.stack.data(combined_stack),
  control.predictor = list(A = inla.stack.A(combined_stack), 
                           compute = TRUE, link = 1),
  family = "pom",
  control.family = list(control.pom = list(cdf = "logit")),
  control.fixed = list(
    mean.intercept = -2,        # Baseline very low bleaching
    prec.intercept = 1,         # Moderate certainty in that belief
    mean = c(dhw = 1, year = 0),
    prec = c(dhw = 0.001, year = 0.001)  # weak dhw prior, weak year prior
  ),
  control.compute =  list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE, 
                          config = TRUE, return.marginals.predictor = TRUE),
  control.inla = list(control.vb = list(emergency = 30)),
  silent=2L
)



# saveRDS(fm1, "/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/data/fm1.rds")
# fm1 <- readRDS("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/data/fm1.rds")

# sp <- inla.spde2.result(fm1, "spatial.field", spde, do.transform = TRUE)
# inla.qmarginal(c(.025,.5,.975), sp$marginals.range.nominal[[1]])   # range, metres
# inla.qmarginal(c(.025,.5,.975), sp$marginals.variance.nominal[[1]]) # marginal variance


##### extract fixed effects

# Extract marginals from fm1$marginals.fixed and convert them into a data frame
combined_df_fixef <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(fm1$marginals.fixed), function(param) {
    df <- as.data.frame(fm1$marginals.fixed[[param]])
    df$parameter <- param
    return(df)
  })
) |>
  rename(x=1, height=2, parameter=3)

# Extract marginals from fm1$marginals.fixed and convert them into a data frame
combined_df_hyper <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(fm1$marginals.hyperpar), function(param) {
    df <- as.data.frame(fm1$marginals.hyperpar[[param]]) # Convert to data frame
    df$parameter <- param # Add a column for the parameter name
    return(df)
  })
) |>
  rename(x=1, height=2, parameter=3) |>
  mutate(parameter = factor(parameter,
                            levels=c("theta1 for POM",
                                     "theta2 for POM",
                                     "theta3 for POM",
                                     "theta4 for POM",
                                     "Theta1 for spatial.field",
                                     "Theta2 for spatial.field")))



# Extract marginals from fm1$marginals.fixed and convert them into a data frame
combined_df_fixef <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(fm1$marginals.fixed), function(param) {
    df <- as.data.frame(fm1$marginals.fixed[[param]])
    df$parameter <- param
    return(df)
  })
) |>
  rename(x = 1, height = 2, parameter = 3) |>
  filter(!parameter == "(Intercept)") |>
  mutate(parameter = factor(parameter, levels = c(
    "dhw", "year2002", "year2016", "year2017", "year2020", "year2022", "year2024", "year2025",
    "dhw:year2002", "dhw:year2016", "dhw:year2017", "dhw:year2020", "dhw:year2022",
    "dhw:year2024","dhw:year2025"
  ), ordered = TRUE))



fixef_plot_ms <- ggplot() + 
  geom_ridgeline(data=combined_df_fixef, 
                 aes(x = x, y = fct_rev(parameter), height = height/10, group = parameter), 
                 fill = "aquamarine4", alpha=0.5) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlim(-3,6) + ylab("") +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0, linewidth=0.5, color="darkgrey") +
  #labs(title = "Posterior Marginals for Fixed Effects", x = "Value (x)", y = "Parameter") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  xlab("Posterior Mean (Effect Size)")



```



```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: false

##### inla proportional predictions


# ordinal (POM) prediction + stacked cumulative-probability figure
# model was fit with control.pom = list(cdf = "logit"), so the back-transform 
# must use plogis(). cut-points are reconstructed from INLA's internal increment
# parameterisation (alpha1 = theta1; alpha_k = alpha_{k-1} + exp(theta_k)),
# i.e.correct ordered-threshold scale 


# ---- 1. POM category probabilities from latent eta + ordered cut-points -----
# P(Y = k) = F(alpha_k - eta) - F(alpha_{k-1} - eta), with F = plogis (logit)
predict_pom_with_bounds <- function(eta_mean, eta_lower, eta_upper, cutpoints,
                                    link = "logit") {
  cdf <- switch(link, logit = plogis, probit = pnorm,
                stop("link must be 'logit' or 'probit'"))
  cutpoints <- sort(cutpoints)                 # ensure ordered thresholds
  n <- length(eta_mean)
  K <- length(cutpoints) + 1L
  
  cat_probs <- function(eta) {
    p <- diff(c(0, cdf(cutpoints - eta), 1))   # K categories
    pmax(p, 0)
  }
  P  <- t(vapply(eta_mean,  cat_probs, numeric(K)))
  Pl <- t(vapply(eta_lower, cat_probs, numeric(K)))
  Pu <- t(vapply(eta_upper, cat_probs, numeric(K)))
  
  list(mean  = as.data.frame(P),
       lower = as.data.frame(pmin(Pl, Pu)),    # CI bounds can invert per category
       upper = as.data.frame(pmax(Pl, Pu)))
}

# ---- 2. reconstruct ORDERED cut-points (not raw thetas) ---------------------
th <- c(fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta1 for POM", "mean"],
        fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta2 for POM", "mean"],
        fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta3 for POM", "mean"],
        fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta4 for POM", "mean"])
cutpoints <- cumsum(c(th[1], exp(th[-1])))     # alpha1, alpha1+exp(th2), ...
# (these should be increasing, e.g. ~ -2.57, 0.46, 1.82)

# ---- 3. use the LATENT LINEAR PREDICTOR, not fitted values ------------------
lp <- fm1$summary.linear.predictor            # <- eta scale, what POM needs
eta_mean  <- lp$mean
eta_lower <- lp$`0.025quant`
eta_upper <- lp$`0.975quant`

pp <- predict_pom_with_bounds(eta_mean, eta_lower, eta_upper, cutpoints,
                              link = "logit")

# ---- 4. attach to the prediction grid ---------------------------------------
index_pred <- inla.stack.index(combined_stack, tag = "preds")$data
point_data <- expanded_grid

predicted_ordinal_mean <- cbind(point_data, pp$mean[index_pred, ]) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("V"), names_to = "aerial_score", values_to = "mean") |>
  mutate(aerial = recode(aerial_score,
                         V1 = "No bleaching", V2 = "Low bleaching",
                         V3 = "Moderate bleaching", V4 = "High bleaching",
                         V5 = "Very high bleaching"),
         aerial = factor(aerial, c("No bleaching","Low bleaching", "Moderate bleaching",
                                   "High bleaching","Very high bleaching")),
         year = as.integer(as.character(year)))

# ---- 5. mask each panel beyond its observed DHW (no extrapolation shown) -----

max_dhw_by_year <- GBRMP_aerial_centroids |> st_drop_geometry() |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(as.character(year))) |>
  group_by(year) |> summarise(min_dhw = ceiling(min(dhw, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                              max_dhw = ceiling(max(dhw, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")

# interpcolors <- c("No bleaching"="#74ADD1","Low-Moderate bleaching"="#FEE090",
#                   "High bleaching"="#FDAE61","Very High-Extreme bleaching"="#D73027")


# <- c("No bleaching"="#ABD9E9","Low-Moderate bleaching"="#FFFFBF",
#                  "High bleaching"="#FDAE61","Very High-Extreme bleaching"="#D73027")

interpcolors_aerial_score_full <- c("#ABD9E9", "#FFFFBF", "#FEE090", "#FDAE61", "#F46D43", "#D73027")
interpcolors_aerial_score_five <- c("#ABD9E9", "#FFFFBF", "#FDAE61", "#F46D43", "#D73027")



plot_with_mask <- function(yr) {
  min_dhw <- max_dhw_by_year |> filter(year == yr) |> pull(min_dhw)
  max_dhw <- max_dhw_by_year |> filter(year == yr) |> pull(max_dhw)
  ggplot() + theme_bw() +
    geom_area(data = predicted_ordinal_mean |> filter(year == yr),
              aes(x = dhw, y = mean, fill = aerial),
              position = "stack", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4,
              show.legend = (yr == 2024)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(2,4,6,8,10,12,14),
               linetype = c("dotted","dotted","dotted","dotdash","dotdash","longdash","longdash"),
               linewidth = 0.2) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = 0, xmax = min_dhw, ymin = 0, ymax = 1), fill = "white", alpha=1) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin = max_dhw, xmax = 16, ymin = 0, ymax = 1), fill = "white") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,16), breaks = seq(0,16,4)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = interpcolors_aerial_score_five) +
    xlab("") + ylab(if (yr == 1998) "Cumulative probability" else "") +
    ggtitle(paste0(yr, " event")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10),
          plot.margin = unit(c(0,0,0,0), "cm"),
          axis.text.y = if (yr == 1998) element_text() else element_blank(),
          legend.position = if (yr == 2024) "bottom" else "none")
}


years <- c(1998,2002,2016,2017,2020,2022,2024, 2025)   # note 2025: DHW floor ~7.5, no low-DHW support
plot_list <- lapply(years, plot_with_mask)

pom_preds <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 8) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.justification = "center")




```

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

##### inla area bars 


# 
# # ----------------------------
# # 1) DHW per polygon-year 
# # ----------------------------
# gbr_reef_32755 <- gbr_reef_subset |>
#   st_make_valid() |>
#   st_transform(32755)
# 
# yrs <- names(OISST_annual_max_dhw)
# 
# reef_year_dhw <- lapply(seq_along(yrs), function(i) {
#   y <- yrs[i]
#   dhw_i <- exactextractr::exact_extract(
#     OISST_annual_max_dhw[[i]],
#     gbr_reef_32755,
#     fun = "max",
#     progress = FALSE
#   )
#   
#   gbr_reef_32755 |>
#     transmute(
#       TARGET_FID = .data$TARGET_FID,
#       LABEL_ID   = .data$LABEL_ID,
#       year       = y,
#       dhw        = dhw_i
#     )
# }) |>
#   bind_rows() |>
#   filter(is.finite(dhw))
# 
# # ----------------------------
# # 2) prediction POINTS for A-matrix (CENTROIDS), but keep keys
# # ----------------------------
# pred_pts <- reef_year_dhw |>
#   st_centroid()
# 
# # build factor levels = training + prediction (so new reefs don't become NA)
# year_levels <- union(levels(GBRMP_aerial_centroids$year), sort(unique(pred_pts$year)))
# id_levels   <- union(levels(factor(GBRMP_aerial_centroids$LABEL_ID)), sort(unique(pred_pts$LABEL_ID)))
# 
# # apply consistent levels to BOTH obs and preds
# GBRMP_aerial_centroids2 <- GBRMP_aerial_centroids |>
#   mutate(
#     year = factor(as.character(year), levels = year_levels),
#     LABEL_ID = factor(as.character(LABEL_ID), levels = id_levels)
#   )
# 
# pred_pts2 <- pred_pts |>
#   mutate(
#     year = factor(as.character(year), levels = year_levels),
#     LABEL_ID = factor(as.character(LABEL_ID), levels = id_levels)
#   )
# 
# A_obs2  <- inla.spde.make.A(mesh = mesh, loc = st_coordinates(GBRMP_aerial_centroids2))
# A_pred2 <- inla.spde.make.A(mesh = mesh, loc = st_coordinates(pred_pts2))
# 
# # ----------------------------
# # 3) stacks + refit (same formula), now with expanded LABEL_ID/year domains
# # ----------------------------
# obs_stack <- inla.stack(
#   data = list(aerial = GBRMP_aerial_centroids2$aerial),
#   A = list(A_obs2, 1),
#   effects = list(
#     c(s_index, list(Intercept = 1)),
#     list(
#       dhw      = GBRMP_aerial_centroids2$dhw,
#       year     = GBRMP_aerial_centroids2$year,
#       LABEL_ID = GBRMP_aerial_centroids2$LABEL_ID
#     )
#   ),
#   tag = "obs"
# )
# 
# pred_stack_poly <- inla.stack(
#   data = list(aerial = NA),
#   A = list(A_pred2, 1),
#   effects = list(
#     c(s_index, list(Intercept = 1)),
#     list(
#       dhw      = pred_pts2$dhw,
#       year     = pred_pts2$year,
#       LABEL_ID = pred_pts2$LABEL_ID
#     )
#   ),
#   tag = "pred_poly"
# )
# 
# stk_poly <- inla.stack(obs_stack, pred_stack_poly)
# 
# inla_formula <- aerial ~ dhw * year + f(spatial.field, model = spde) + f(LABEL_ID, model = "iid")
# 
# fm_poly <- inla(
#   inla_formula,
#   data = inla.stack.data(stk_poly),
#   control.predictor = list(A = inla.stack.A(stk_poly), compute = TRUE, link = 1),
#   family = "pom",
#   control.family = list(control.pom = list(cdf = "logit")),
#   control.compute = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE, config = TRUE, return.marginals.predictor = TRUE),
#   control.inla = list(control.vb = list(emergency = 30)),
#   silent = 2L
# )
# 
# # ----------------------------
# # 4) extract polygon-year predictions + probs
# # ----------------------------
# idx <- inla.stack.index(stk_poly, tag = "pred_poly")$data
# 
# idx_curve <- inla.stack.index(combined_stack, "preds")$data
# idx_poly  <- inla.stack.index(combined_stack, "pred_poly")$data
# 
# cutpoints <- c(
#   fm_poly$summary.hyperpar["theta1 for POM", "mean"],
#   fm_poly$summary.hyperpar["theta2 for POM", "mean"],
#   fm_poly$summary.hyperpar["theta3 for POM", "mean"]
# )
# 
# eta_mean <- fm_poly$summary.fitted.values$mean[idx]
# eta_lo   <- fm_poly$summary.fitted.values$`0.025quant`[idx]
# eta_hi   <- fm_poly$summary.fitted.values$`0.975quant`[idx]
# 
# p_mean <- pom_probs(eta_mean, cutpoints) |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_mean"))
# p_lo   <- pom_probs(eta_lo,   cutpoints) |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_lo"))
# p_hi   <- pom_probs(eta_hi,   cutpoints) |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_hi"))
# 
# pred_probs_poly_pts <- bind_cols(
#   pred_pts2 |>
#     st_drop_geometry() |>
#     select(TARGET_FID, LABEL_ID, year, dhw),
#   p_mean, p_lo, p_hi
# ) |>
#   mutate(
#     p345_mean = p3_mean + p4_mean,
#     p345_lo   = p3_lo   + p4_lo,
#     p345_hi   = p3_hi   + p4_hi,
#     cat_mode  = c("1","2","3","4")[max.col(cbind(p1_mean, p2_mean, p3_mean, p4_mean), ties.method = "first")]
#   )
# 
# # ----------------------------
# # 5) APPLY BACK TO POLYGONS (polygon-year sf)  ✅ no NA years
# # ----------------------------
# gbr_pred_sf <- reef_year_dhw |>
#   left_join(pred_probs_poly_pts, by = c("TARGET_FID", "LABEL_ID", "year", "dhw")) |>
#   st_transform(4326)

```


```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

##### inla raw spatial interp

library(dplyr)
library(sf)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# === polygon probabilities FROM fm1 (correct scale + cut-points) ===
alpha <- with(fm1$summary.hyperpar, {
  th <- fm1$summary.hyperpar[grep("theta.*POM", rownames(fm1$summary.hyperpar)), "mean"]
  cumsum(c(th[1], exp(th[-1])))                       # ordered cut-points
})

pom_probs <- function(eta, alpha) {                   # eta = LINEAR PREDICTOR
  s <- plogis(outer(alpha, eta, `-`))                 # 3 x n cumulative
  tibble(p1 = s[1,], p2 = s[2,] - s[1,], p3 = s[3,] - s[2,], p4 = 1 - s[3,])
}

idx_poly <- inla.stack.index(combined_stack, "pred_poly")$data
lp <- fm1$summary.linear.predictor[idx_poly, ]        # LATENT scale

p_mean <- pom_probs(lp$mean,          alpha) |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_mean"))
p_lo   <- pom_probs(lp$`0.025quant`,  alpha) |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_lo"))
p_hi   <- pom_probs(lp$`0.975quant`,  alpha) |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_hi"))

pred_probs_poly_pts <- bind_cols(
  pred_pts2 |> st_drop_geometry() |> select(TARGET_FID, LABEL_ID, year, dhw),
  p_mean, p_lo, p_hi)

gbr_pred_sf <- reef_year_dhw |>
  left_join(pred_probs_poly_pts, by = c("TARGET_FID","LABEL_ID","year","dhw"))

# --- reef area in km^2 for each polygon-year row (keep geometry out) ---
gbr_pred_sf_area <- gbr_pred_sf |>
  st_make_valid() |> st_transform(32755) |>
  mutate(poly_area_km2 = as.numeric(st_area(geometry)) / 1e6) |>
  st_drop_geometry()

# --- expected area in each category per year (using probabilities) ---
area_year_cat <- gbr_pred_sf_area |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(as.character(year))) |>
  transmute(year,
            `1` = poly_area_km2 * p1_mean, `2` = poly_area_km2 * p2_mean,
            `3` = poly_area_km2 * p3_mean, `4` = poly_area_km2 * p4_mean) |>
  pivot_longer(c(`1`,`2`,`3`,`4`), names_to = "aerial_score", values_to = "area_km2") |>
  group_by(year, aerial_score) |>
  summarise(area_km2 = sum(area_km2, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(aerial_score = factor(aerial_score, levels = c("1","2","3","4")))

fill_vals <- c(
  "5" = "#D73027",
  "4" = "#F46D43",
  "3" = "#FDAE61",
  "2" = "#FFFFFF",
  "1" = "#ABD9E9"
)



fill_labs <- c(
  "1" = "No bleaching",
  "2" = "Low bleaching",
  "3" = "Moderate bleaching",
  "4" = "High bleaching",
  "5" = "Severe bleaching"
)

inla_spatial_preds <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_bar(
    data = area_year_cat,
    aes(x = factor(year), y = area_km2, fill = aerial_score),
    stat = "identity",
    position = "stack",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.2,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "",
    values = fill_vals,
    labels = fill_labs
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = expression(italic(A) ~ "(km"^2*")")
  ) +
#  theme(legend.position = c(0.12, 0.85)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE))


inla_spatial_predsarea_year_cat_prop <- area_year_cat |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    total_area_km2 = sum(area_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_area_km2  = dplyr::if_else(total_area_km2 > 0, area_km2 / total_area_km2, NA_real_)
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(aerial_score %in% c(2, 3, 4)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    prop_area_km2 = dplyr::if_else(
      sum(!is.na(prop_area_km2)) > 0,
      sum(prop_area_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
      NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


# sidepanel <-   fixef_plot_ms | inla_spatial_preds
# 
# combined_plots

```


```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-height: 9
#| fig-width: 12
#| cache: false



# model-driven: mapped P(severe bleaching) per event from fm1
#
# interpolation of RAW aerial scores with the model posterior
# prediction surface: P(severe) = 1 - F(alpha2 - eta), F = plogis (logit POM),
# evaluated at every prediction-grid cell, per event year. The spatial SPDE field
# does the interpolation (informed by neighbours + DHW + year), and we can map
# the posterior MEAN and its 95% width (uncertainty) 
# =============================================================================


# ---- 1. ordered cut-points (alpha) on the logit latent scale ----------------
th <- c(fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta1 for POM","mean"],
        fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta2 for POM","mean"],
        fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta3 for POM","mean"],
        fm1$summary.hyperpar["theta4 for POM","mean"])
alpha <- cumsum(c(th[1], exp(th[-1])))          # e.g. -2.44, 0.65, 2.02
alpha3 <- alpha[3]                              # threshold into the SEVERE class

# ---- 2. latent linear predictor at the prediction cells ---------------------
idx <- inla.stack.index(combined_stack, tag = "preds")$data
lp  <- fm1$summary.linear.predictor[idx, c("mean","0.025quant","0.975quant")]

# P(severe) = 1 - F(alpha3 - eta); monotone in eta so CI maps through the bounds
p_sev   <- 1 - plogis(alpha3 - lp$mean)
p_sev_lo<- 1 - plogis(alpha3 - lp$`0.025quant`)
p_sev_hi<- 1 - plogis(alpha3 - lp$`0.975quant`)

pred_map <- expanded_grid |>
  mutate(p_severe = p_sev,
         p_lo = pmin(p_sev_lo, p_sev_hi),
         p_hi = pmax(p_sev_lo, p_sev_hi),
         p_width = p_hi - p_lo,                 # posterior uncertainty
         year = as.integer(as.character(year)))

# --- rebuild a CELL-level prediction stack (one row per grid cell per year) ---
GBR_grid_pred <- GBR_grid |>                    # hex grid sf: geometry, year, dhw
  mutate(year = factor(year, levels = c(1998,2002,2016,2017,2020,2022,2024,2025)))
coords_cells <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(GBR_grid_pred))
A_cells <- inla.spde.make.A(mesh, loc = coords_cells)

stk_cells <- inla.stack(
  data = list(aerial = NA),
  A = list(A_cells, 1),
  effects = list(c(s_index, list(Intercept = 1)),
                 list(dhw = GBR_grid_pred$dhw, year = GBR_grid_pred$year, LABEL_ID = NA)),
  tag = "cellmap")

# re-predict by stacking cells with the obs and using
# fm1's config to sample, OR (simplest) re-run inla with the cell stack appended.

full_stk <- inla.stack(obs_stack, stk_cells)

fm_map <- inla(
  inla_formula, family = "pom",
  data = inla.stack.data(full_stk),
  control.family = list(control.pom = list(cdf = "logit")),
  control.predictor = list(A = inla.stack.A(full_stk), compute = TRUE, link = 1),
  control.fixed = list(mean.intercept = -2, prec.intercept = 1,
                       mean = c(dhw = 0, year = 0), prec = c(dhw = 0.001, year = 0.001)),
  control.compute = list(return.marginals.predictor = FALSE),   # keep it light
  control.mode = list(result = fm1, restart = FALSE),           # reuse fm1's mode -> fast
  control.inla = list(control.vb = list(emergency = 30)), silent = 2L)

ci <- inla.stack.index(full_stk, tag = "cellmap")$data
lpc <- fm_map$summary.linear.predictor[ci, c("mean","0.025quant","0.975quant")]

GBR_grid_pred <- GBR_grid_pred |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(as.character(year)),
         p_severe = 1 - plogis(alpha3 - lpc$mean),
         p_lo = 1 - plogis(alpha3 - lpc$`0.025quant`),
         p_hi = 1 - plogis(alpha3 - lpc$`0.975quant`),
         p_width = pmax(p_lo,p_hi) - pmin(p_lo,p_hi))

# save for sup p severe plot
saveRDS(GBR_grid_pred, "~/GBR-dhw/data/GBR_grid_pred.rds")
saveRDS(GBRMPpolygon, "~/GBR-dhw/data/GBRMPpolygon_grid_pred.rds")


# supplementary
# re-extract latent predictor at the cell-map cells 
if (!exists("lpc")) {
  ci  <- inla.stack.index(full_stk, tag = "cellmap")$data
  lpc <- fm_map$summary.linear.predictor[ci, c("mean","0.025quant","0.975quant")]
}
 
# latent-scale 95% CI width = pure spatial information (no probability squashing)
GBR_grid_pred_sup <- GBR_grid_pred |>
  mutate(eta_ci_width = lpc$`0.975quant` - lpc$`0.025quant`,
         year = as.integer(as.character(year)))
 

saveRDS(GBR_grid_pred_sup, "~/GBR-dhw/data/GBR_grid_pred.rds")



```

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(cowplot)
library(scales)

GBR_grid_pred_plot <- GBR_grid_pred |> 
  st_filter(GBRMPpolygon, .predicate = st_intersects)


#sev_pal <- c("#74ADD1","#FFFFBF","#FDAE61","#F46D43","#D73027")  # blue->red
sev_pal <- c("#ABD9E9", "#FFFFBF", "#FEE090", "#FDAE61", "#F46D43",  "#D73027")

gbr_pSevere_1998 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==1998), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=ausmap |> st_transform(4326), fill="olivedrab4", 
          alpha=0.2, color="black", linewidth=0.2) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, 
                                margin = margin(b = -1)),
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
    ) + ggtitle("1998")

gbr_pSevere_2002 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2002), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2002")


gbr_pSevere_2016 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2016), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2016")


gbr_pSevere_2017 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2017), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2017")

gbr_pSevere_2020 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2020), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2020")

gbr_pSevere_2022 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2022), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2022")

gbr_pSevere_2024 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2024), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2024")

gbr_pSevere_2025 <- ggplot() +# theme_gbr +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2025), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, color="black", fill=NA) +
  coord_sf(lims_method = "geometry_bbox") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) + ggtitle("2025")

legend_psevere_only <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data=GBRMPpolygon, linewidth=0.25, fill=NA) +
  geom_sf(data = GBR_grid_pred_plot |> filter(year==2025), 
          aes(fill = p_severe), show.legend=TRUE) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = sev_pal, limits = c(0,1),
                       name = "P(severe)", labels = percent) +
  theme_void() +
   theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.11, vjust = -0.5, margin = margin(b = -1)),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  )  +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "P"))

legend_psevere_only <- cowplot::get_legend(legend_psevere_only) |> cowplot::plot_grid()

plotmin=-0.35
psevere_plot_panel <- ggdraw(xlim = c(0, 4), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_1998, plotmin+0.3, 0, 1, 1) +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_2002, plotmin+0.7, 0, 1, 1) +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_2016, plotmin+1.1, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_2017, plotmin+1.5, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_2020, plotmin+1.9, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_2022, plotmin+2.3, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_2024, plotmin+2.7, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(gbr_pSevere_2025, plotmin+3.1, 0, 1, 1)  +
  draw_plot(legend_psevere_only, plotmin+3.3, 0.125, 1, 1)




```





```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: false

##### inla spatial proportional predictions

# Model-driven map: MODAL POM CATEGORY per cell (categorical "interval" map)
# For each cell compute all four class probabilities from the latent eta and
# the ordered cut-points, take the most-probable class (which.max), and map that
# as a discrete fill


# ---- ordered cut-points (3 cut-points -> 4 classes) -------------------------
th    <- fm1$summary.hyperpar[grep("theta.*POM", rownames(fm1$summary.hyperpar)), "mean"]
alpha <- cumsum(c(th[1], exp(th[-1])))          # -2.3091588 -0.4769497  0.8163303  2.1877035

# ---- class probabilities from latent eta ------------------------------------
# P(Y=k) = F(alpha_k - eta) - F(alpha_{k-1} - eta); F = plogis (logit POM)
# pom_probs <- function(eta, alpha) {
#   s <- plogis(outer(alpha, eta, `-`))           # 3 x n cumulative
#   cbind(p1 = s[1, ],
#         p2 = s[2, ] - s[1, ],
#         p3 = s[3, ] - s[2, ],
#         p4 = 1 - s[3, ])
# }

pom_probs <- function(eta, alpha) {
  s <- plogis(outer(alpha, eta, `-`))           # 5 x n cumulative
  cbind(p1 = s[1, ],
        p2 = s[2, ] - s[1, ],
        p3 = s[3, ] - s[2, ],
        p4 = s[4, ] - s[3, ],
        p5 = 1 - s[4, ])
}

# ---- cell-level prediction stack (one row per grid cell per year) -----------
GBR_grid_pred <- GBR_grid |>
  mutate(year = factor(year, levels = c(1998,2002,2016,2017,2020,2022,2024,2025)))
coords_cells <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(GBR_grid_pred))
A_cells <- inla.spde.make.A(mesh, loc = coords_cells)

stk_cells <- inla.stack(
  data = list(aerial = NA),
  A = list(A_cells, 1),
  effects = list(c(s_index, list(Intercept = 1)),
                 list(dhw = GBR_grid_pred$dhw, year = GBR_grid_pred$year, LABEL_ID = NA)),
  tag = "cellmap")

full_stk <- inla.stack(obs_stack, stk_cells)

fm_map <- inla(
  inla_formula, family = "pom",
  data = inla.stack.data(full_stk),
  control.family = list(control.pom = list(cdf = "logit")),
  control.predictor = list(A = inla.stack.A(full_stk), compute = TRUE, link = 1),
  control.fixed = list(mean.intercept = -2, prec.intercept = 1,
                       mean = c(dhw = 0, year = 0), prec = c(dhw = 0.001, year = 0.001)),
  control.compute = list(return.marginals.predictor = FALSE),
  control.mode = list(result = fm1, restart = FALSE),
  control.inla = list(control.vb = list(emergency = 30)), silent = 2L)

ci  <- inla.stack.index(full_stk, tag = "cellmap")$data
eta <- fm_map$summary.linear.predictor[ci, "mean"]      # latent predictor

# ---- modal class + its probability ------------------------------------------
P <- pom_probs(eta, alpha)
GBR_grid_pred_pom <- GBR_grid_pred |>
  mutate(year     = as.integer(as.character(year)),
         cat_mode = factor(c("1","2","3","4")[max.col(P, ties.method = "first")],
                           levels = c("1","2","3","4")),
         p_mode   = apply(P, 1, max),                    # confidence in modal class
         p_severe = P[, "p4"])                           # keep if you still want it

saveRDS(GBRMPpolygon, "~/GBR-dhw/data/GBRMPpolygon.rds")
saveRDS(GBR_grid_pred_pom, "~/GBR-dhw/data/GBR_grid_pred_pom.rds")


# bleaching 8DHW thresholds
# fe <- setNames(fm1$summary.fixed[, "mean"], rownames(fm1$summary.fixed))
# b0 <- as.numeric(fe["(Intercept)"])
# bd <- as.numeric(fe["dhw"])
# yint <- function(y) if (y == 1998) 0 else as.numeric(fe[paste0("year", y)])
# yslp <- function(y) if (y == 1998) 0 else as.numeric(fe[paste0("dhw:year", y)])
# 
# alpha <- { th <- fm1$summary.hyperpar[grep("theta.*POM", rownames(fm1$summary.hyperpar)), "mean"]
# cumsum(c(th[1], exp(th[-1]))) }
# 
# curve_8DHW_threshold <- expand_grid(year = c(1998,2002,2016,2017,2020,2022,2024,2025),
#                          dhw = seq(0, 16, 0.25)) |>
#   rowwise() |>
#   # POM cut-point mapping
#     # alpha[1] → P(Y ≥ 2) = any bleaching (mild-or-worse)
#     # alpha[2] → P(Y ≥ 3) = moderate-or-worse
#     # alpha[3] → P(Y ≥ 4) = severe only
#   mutate(eta   = b0 + yint(year) + (bd + yslp(year)) * dhw,
#          p_thresh = plogis(eta - alpha[3])) |>
#   ungroup()
# ggplot(curve_ge3, aes(dhw, p_ge3, colour = factor(year))) +
#   geom_line() + geom_vline(xintercept = 8) + theme_bw()


# scatter consistent WITH the curve facets (same lattice, same averaging)
dhwexeedance <- predicted_ordinal_mean |>
  #filter(!aerial_score %in% c("V2","V3","V4"), dhw == 8) |>      
  filter(aerial_score %in% c("V3", "V4"), dhw == 8) |>      
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(p = mean(mean), .groups = "drop") |>   # average across cells, as curves do
  ggplot() + theme_bw() +
  geom_point(aes(year, p, fill=factor(year)), shape=21, size=3, show.legend=FALSE ) + 
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(1998,2026), breaks=seq(1998,2026, 8), minor_breaks = NULL)  +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,1), breaks=c(0, 0.5, 1), minor_breaks = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=year_colors, name="year") +
  ylab("P exceeding very-high \nbleaching @ 8DHW")

# 
# predicted_ordinal_mean |>
#   filter(dhw == 8) |>      
#   group_by(year, aerial_score) |>
#   summarise(p = mean(mean), .groups = "drop") |>   # average across cells, as curves do
#   ggplot() + theme_bw() +
#   geom_smooth(aes(year, p, fill=factor(aerial_score), color=factor(aerial_score)), method="lm") + 
#   scale_x_continuous(limits=c(1998,2026), breaks=seq(1998,2026, 8), minor_breaks = NULL)  +
#   scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,1), breaks=c(0, 0.5, 1), minor_breaks = NULL)

index_pred <- inla.stack.index(combined_stack, tag = "preds")$data

preds8 <- bind_rows(
  cbind(expanded_grid, pp$mean[index_pred, ],  bound = "mean"),
  cbind(expanded_grid, pp$lower[index_pred, ], bound = "lo"),
  cbind(expanded_grid, pp$upper[index_pred, ], bound = "hi")
) |>
  filter(dhw == 8) |>
  pivot_longer(starts_with("V"), names_to = "aerial_score", values_to = "p") |>
  group_by(year, aerial_score, bound) |>
  summarise(p = mean(p), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = bound, values_from = p)




# 
# ggplot(preds8, aes(year, mean, colour=aerial_score, group=aerial_score)) +
#   geom_pointrange(aes(ymin=lo, ymax=hi), position=position_dodge(0.8)) +
#   #geom_line(alpha=0.4) +                          # connect years, NOT a regression
#   scale_colour_manual(values=interpcolors_aerial_score, name="Bleaching class") +
#   scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,1)) + theme_bw() +
#   ylab("P(class) at 8 DHW") + facet_grid(~aerial_score)
# 
# dhwexeedance <- curve_8DHW_threshold |> filter(dhw==8) |>
#   ggplot() + theme_bw() +
#   geom_point(aes(year, p_thresh, fill=factor(year)), shape=21, size=3, show.legend=FALSE) +
#   scale_x_continuous(limits=c(1998,2026), breaks=seq(1998,2026, 8), minor_breaks = NULL)  +
#   scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,1), breaks=c(0, 0.5, 1), minor_breaks = NULL) +
#   scale_fill_manual(values=year_colors, name="year") +
#   ylab("P exceeding very-high \nbleaching @ 8DHW")
# 
# 

library(brms)
library(tidybayes)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(patchwork)

fixed_dhw <- 8

lab_levels <- c("No", "Low", "Moderate", "High", "Very high")

interpcolors_aerial_score <- c(
  "No" = "#ABD9E9",
  "Low" = "#FFFFBF",
  "Moderate" = "#FDAE61",
  "High" = "#F46D43",
  "Very high" = "#D73027"
  )

cat_labs <- setNames(lab_levels, as.character(1:5))


target_years <- c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025)

obs <- GBRMP_aerial_centroids |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  mutate(
    year_num = as.integer(as.character(year)),
    year_c = year_num - 2012,
    aerial = ordered(aerial, levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5))
  ) |>
  filter(
    year_num != 2025,
    is.finite(dhw),
    !is.na(aerial)
  )

ctrl <- list(adapt_delta = 0.95)

pr_b <- prior(normal(0, 2), class = "b")
# 
# m_spl <- brm(
#   aerial ~ s(year_num, k = 3) + dhw,
#   data = obs,
#   family = cumulative("logit"),
#   prior = pr_b,
#   chains = 4,
#   cores = 4,
#   iter = 2000,
#   seed = 1,
#   control = ctrl,
#   backend = "rstan"
# )
# 
# saveRDS(m_spl, "~/GBR-dhw/outputs/m_spl.rds")
m_spl <- readRDS("~/GBR-dhw/outputs/m_spl.rds")

nd <- tibble(
  year_num = seq(1998, 2024, length.out = 100),
  dhw = fixed_dhw
) |>
  mutate(year_c = year_num - 2012)

ed_df <- nd |>
  add_epred_draws(m_spl, category = "aerial") |>
  rename(cat = aerial) |>
  group_by(year_num, .draw) |>
  arrange(desc(as.integer(cat)), .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(p_ge = cumsum(.epred)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    cat_lab = factor(cat_labs[as.character(cat)], levels = lab_levels)
  )

closest_years <- ed_df |>
  distinct(year_num) |>
  crossing(target_year = target_years) |>
  mutate(diff = abs(year_num - target_year)) |>
  slice_min(diff, by = target_year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  select(target_year, year_num)

mean_ed_df <- ed_df |>
  group_by(year_num, cat_lab) |>
  summarise(
    p_ge = median(p_ge),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

events <- closest_years |>
  filter(target_year <= 2024)

event_years <- c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024)

brms_exceedance_plot <- mean_ed_df |>
  ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_area(
    aes(year_num, p_ge, colour = cat_lab, fill = cat_lab),
    position = "identity",
    color="black",
    linewidth=0.5,
    show.legend=FALSE,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = tibble(year_num = event_years),
    aes(
      x = year_num,
      xend = year_num,
      y = 0,
      yend = 1
    ),
    linetype = "dashed",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = interpcolors_aerial_score, name = "≥ class") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = interpcolors_aerial_score, name = "≥ class") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(1998, 2026),
    breaks = seq(1998, 2026, 4)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = paste0("P(Y ≥ class) @ ", fixed_dhw, " DHW")
  )





```

#### Figure 2


```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| out-width: 100%
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-width: 15
#| fig-cap: |
#|   **Figure 2 | Spatial thermal stress, bleaching response and the changing
#|   dose–response across eight Great Barrier Reef mass-bleaching events
#|   (1998–2025).**
#|   (**A**) Maximum annual Degree Heating Weeks (DHWmax, °C-weeks; top row) and
#|   observed aerial bleaching score (bottom row) for each event. Thermal stress
#|   intensified and expanded over time, with the highest DHWmax (>15–21 °C-weeks)
#|   concentrated in the northern Great Barrier Reef during 2016, 2020, 2024 and
#|   2025. Aerial scores follow standard rapid-assessment classes (None <1%, Low
#|   1–10%, Medium 11–30%, High 31–60%, Very high 61–90%, Extreme >91%); grey areas
#|   were not surveyed. Note the restricted coverage and absence of low-DHW reefs
#|   in 2025. (**B**) Reef area (km²) and number of reefs impacted by each bleaching category 
#|   per event, with higher categories increasing substantially across recent events 
#|   (note 2025 is #| limited in extent largely due to limited survey area, see (A). 
#|   (**C**) Posterior marginal distributions of fixed effects
#|   (logit scale; 1998 reference). Year terms estimate baseline bleaching
#|   propensity at reference thermal stress and rise markedly in recent events
#|   (2020, 2022, 2024), with credible intervals excluding zero; DHW × year
#|   interactions are small and mixed in sign, indicating the shift is driven by
#|   rising baseline propensity rather than a uniform change in dose sensitivity.
#|   Estimates are net of the regional-scale spatial field (Methods). 
#|   (**D**) Posterior cumulative-probability distributions of bleaching
#|   class across the DHW gradient (0–16 °C-weeks) for each event, from the
#|   Bayesian spatial proportional-odds model; bands show the probability of each
#|   ordered class (no, mild, moderate, severe), truncated at each event's maximum
#|   observed DHW (2025 surveyed only above ~7.5 °C-weeks). The progressive
#|   leftward expansion of the severe band, and contraction of the no-bleaching
#|   band even at low DHW, indicate Very high bleaching (>60%) now occurs at progressively
#|   lower thermal dose. (**E**) Model-estimated probability of exceeding each bleaching 
#|   class at a fixed thermal dose (8 °C-weeks DHW) across events, from the ordinal model 
#|   with a smooth (penalised spline) temporal term. Lines show the posterior median probability 
#|   that a reef equals or exceeds the Low–Moderate, High, and Very high–Extreme 
#|   classes; shaded areas denote the region below each exceedance curve. Holding 
#|   thermal dose constant, the probability of exceeding the higher bleaching 
#|   classes rose substantially across the record — P(≥ Very high–Extreme) increasing 
#|   from ~0.15 in 1998 to ~0.7 in 2024 — demonstrating that for an equivalent level 
#|   of heat stress, severe bleaching has become markedly more likely over time 
#|   (contextual vulnerability)

library(patchwork)
library(grid)

years <- c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025)

plot_list <- lapply(years, plot_with_mask)

pom_preds <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 8) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(
    legend.position = "right"
  )

map_panel_core <- (
  dhw_plot_panel /
    aerial_plot_panel +
    plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1), guides = "collect")
) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1)
  )

map_panel <- wrap_elements(full = map_panel_core)

fixef_b <- fixef_plot_ms +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
  )

aerial_year_area_plot <- aerial_year_area +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
  )

bottom_left <- wrap_elements(full = pom_preds) +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(2, 26, 2, 2)
  )

bottom_right <- brms_exceedance_plot +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 18)
  )

layout_design <- c(
  area(t = 1, l = 1, b = 2, r = 3), # A
  area(t = 1, l = 4, b = 1, r = 4), # B
  area(t = 2, l = 4, b = 2, r = 4), # C
  area(t = 3, l = 1, b = 3, r = 3), # D
  area(t = 3, l = 4, b = 3, r = 4)  # E
)

final_plot <- wrap_plots(
  map_panel,
  aerial_year_area_plot,
  fixef_b,
  bottom_left,
  bottom_right,
  design = layout_design,
  widths = c(1, 1, 1, 1),
  heights = c(0.325, 0.325, 0.25)
) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = list(c("(A)", "(B)", "(C)", "(D)", "(E)"))
  ) &
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.35, "lines"),
    plot.tag = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    plot.tag.position = c(0, 1)
  )

final_plot



# ggsave(final_plot, filename="~/Desktop/combined.pdf", dpi="retina", width=15, height=9, units="in", scale=1)



```

### Mass bleaching in the context of a century of climate variability

To place the recent events in their full historical context, we applied the fitted spatial proportional-odds model to the reconstructed 1940–2025 thermal record, predicting the bleaching each reef would have experienced under the heat of every year in the reconstruction. Because the model's year effects are estimated relative to 1998, the earliest event in the aerial record, we held the year contribution fixed at this reference level for all hindcast years. Each historical prediction therefore represents a deliberate counterfactual: the bleaching that a reef's reconstructed thermal stress would have produced had reef sensitivity remained at its earliest-observed state, rather than at the elevated sensitivity of recent events. This isolates the contribution of thermal dose alone, before the contextual increase in vulnerability documented above, and provides a conservative baseline against which the modern era can be judged. Throughout, we summarise predicted bleaching as the number of reefs reaching high or greater severity, corresponding to more than 30% of the reef bleached.

Under this reference-sensitivity hindcast, severe bleaching was rare across the twentieth century (Fig. 3a,b). Predicted high-or-greater bleaching remained low and episodic from 1940 through the late 1990s, with even strongly positive years producing limited reef-wide impact, and no historical year approaches the extent predicted for the recent events. From 2016 onward the predicted extent rises several-fold above any earlier year, with the largest values concentrated in recent events (2016, 2020, 2022 and 2024). Mass bleaching has been variously defined (Hughes et al 2018, de Carlo 2024) as "regional" in scale", occurring across the length of the GBR. Spatial mapping indicates that the hind cast bleaching events were largely limited in scale to local events within regions (e.g. 1964, 1986, 1992), while moderate events (e.g. 1970, 1982) span multiple regions and align closer to the definition of a "mass bleaching" footprint with low impacts, supporting earlier interpretations of mass bleaching events prior to 1998 (de Carlo 2024), albiet at a lower intensity and reduced footprint to the recent intensification. The past decade is unprecedented in impact and spatial scale, with high bleaching across a far greater footprint with progressively southward impact from the earlier northern focus (Fig. 2c). That this contrast emerges even when reef sensitivity is held at its 1998 level is notable, because it shows the escalation is driven in the first instance by the reconstructed rise in thermal stress itself; the additional increase in contextual vulnerability since 2016 acts on top of an already unprecedented thermal signal, compounding rather than causing the departure from twentieth-century climatic regime.

Severe coral bleaching has historically been associated with El Niño conditions, when the warm phase of the oscillation elevates regional sea-surface temperatures, and the higher-anomaly years in the record broadly conform to this expectation (Fig. 3d). The recent events weaken the association. Although 2016 and 2024 coincided with strong positive anomalies, the 2022 event ranked among the most severe of all eight despite occurring under the strongest La Niña anomaly in the record. Grouping events by ENSO sign, positive-anomaly and negative-anomaly events spanned almost the same severity range (0 to 5.5 and 0 to 5.2 respectively), and although positive years carried a higher median severity, per-event severity since 1998 showed no statistical association with the Niño 3.4 anomaly once thermal dose and spatial structure were accounted for (weighted regression, slope 0.48 [95% CI −1.44, 2.39], R² = 0.08). ENSO phase therefore no longer reliably separates severe events from mild ones: background ocean warming appears to have raised baseline thermal stress to the point where a strong El Niño is no longer a prerequisite for mass bleaching. The oscillation still modulates which years are hottest, but no longer determines whether severe bleaching occurs, a shift with no clear precedent in the twentieth-century record.

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: false
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(dhw)

# 
# GBR_OISST2_DHW_annual_max <- rast("/Users/rof011/GBR-dhw/datasets/GBR_OISST2_DHW.tif") |>
#   summarise_raster(index = "years", fun = max, na.rm = TRUE, overwrite=TRUE)
# 
# 
# years <- names(GBR_OISST2_DHW_annual_max) |>
#   str_remove("^y_") |>
#   as.integer()
# 
# layer_tbl <- tibble(
#   layer = seq_len(nlyr(GBR_OISST2_DHW_annual_max)),
#   name = names(GBR_OISST2_DHW_annual_max),
#   year = str_remove(name, "^y_") |> as.integer()
# ) |>
#   distinct(year, .keep_all = TRUE)
# 
# gbr_shape_aerial_OISST2 <- layer_tbl %>%
#   split(.$year) |>
#   map_dfr(\(x) {
#     extract_reefs(
#       GBR_OISST2_DHW_annual_max[[x$layer]],
#       gbr_reefs,
#       varname = "dhw"
#     ) |>
#       mutate(year = x$year)
#   }) |>
#   select(-date)

#saveRDS(gbr_shape_aerial_OISST2, "~/GBR-dhw/datasets/gbr_shape_aerial_OISST2.rds")
gbr_shape_aerial_OISST2 <- readRDS("~/GBR-dhw/datasets/gbr_shape_aerial_OISST2.rds")

library(sf) 
library(INLA)
library(tidyverse)

## ============================================================
## shared pieces
## ============================================================
fe  <- setNames(fm1$summary.fixed[, "mean"], rownames(fm1$summary.fixed))
b0  <- fe[["(Intercept)"]]
bd  <- fe[["dhw"]]

field_mean <- fm1$summary.random$spatial.field$mean
iid_tab    <- setNames(fm1$summary.random$LABEL_ID$mean,
                       fm1$summary.random$LABEL_ID$ID)

th    <- fm1$summary.hyperpar[grepl("theta.*POM", rownames(fm1$summary.hyperpar),
                                    ignore.case = TRUE), "mean"]
alpha <- cumsum(c(th[1], exp(th[-1])))                 # 4 thresholds -> 5 cats
stopifnot(length(alpha) == 4)

pom_probs5 <- function(eta, alpha){
  s <- plogis(outer(alpha, eta, `-`))                  # 4 x n cumulative
  t(rbind(p1 = s[1, ],
          p2 = s[2, ] - s[1, ],
          p3 = s[3, ] - s[2, ],
          p4 = s[4, ] - s[3, ],
          p5 = 1      - s[4, ]))                        # -> n x 5
}

# field + iid for an sf of reef points; iid=0 where LABEL_ID unseen
field_iid <- function(sf_pts){
  xy    <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(sf_pts))
  field <- as.numeric(inla.spde.make.A(mesh, xy) %*% field_mean)
  iid   <- iid_tab[as.character(sf_pts$LABEL_ID)]; iid[is.na(iid)] <- 0
  list(field = field, iid = as.numeric(iid))
}

yint <- function(y) if (y == 1998) 0 else fe[[paste0("year", y)]]
yslp <- function(y) if (y == 1998) 0 else fe[[paste0("dhw:year", y)]]

# turn an eta vector + sf into a labelled prediction sf
attach_preds <- function(sf_pts, eta){
  P <- pom_probs5(eta, alpha)
  sf_pts |>
    mutate(eta = eta,
           p1 = P[,1], p2 = P[,2], p3 = P[,3], p4 = P[,4], p5 = P[,5],
           `P(>=Low)`      = 1 - p1,
           `P(>=Moderate)` = p3 + p4 + p5,
           `P(>=High)`     = p4 + p5,
           `P(VeryHigh)`   = p5,
           cat_mode = factor(max.col(P, ties.method = "first"),
                             levels = 1:5,
                             labels = c("No","Low","Moderate","High","Very high")))
}

## ============================================================
## 1a. HINDCAST  (1941–1997): year term zeroed = 1998-era dose-response
## ============================================================
pred_hind <- gbr_shape_aerial_OISST2 |>
  filter(year >= 1941, year <= 1997) |>
  st_make_valid() |> st_transform(32755)

fi_h  <- field_iid(pred_hind)
eta_h <- b0 + fi_h$field + fi_h$iid + bd * pred_hind$dhw   # no year terms

fm1_hind <- attach_preds(pred_hind, eta_h) |> mutate(era = "hindcast")

## ============================================================
## 1b. HINDCAST modern-mean response:
##     1941–1997 DHW, but average contemporary year/dhw effect
## ============================================================

modern_years <- c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025)

modern_year_terms <- tibble(
  year = modern_years,
  year_int = map_dbl(year, yint),
  year_slp = map_dbl(year, yslp)
)

modern_mean_int <- mean(modern_year_terms$year_int, na.rm = TRUE)
modern_mean_slp <- mean(modern_year_terms$year_slp, na.rm = TRUE)

fi_h2 <- field_iid(pred_hind)

eta_h2 <- b0 +
  fi_h2$field +
  fi_h2$iid +
  modern_mean_int +
  (bd + modern_mean_slp) * pred_hind$dhw

fm1_hind2 <- attach_preds(pred_hind, eta_h2) |>
  mutate(era = "hindcast_modern_mean")

## ============================================================
## 2. CONTEMPORARY (1998–2025 events): full eta, real year + dhw
## ============================================================
pred_con <- gbr_shape_aerial_OISST2 |>
  filter(year %in% c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025)) |>
  st_make_valid() |> st_transform(32755)

fi_c  <- field_iid(pred_con)
yr_c  <- as.integer(as.character(pred_con$year))
eta_c <- b0 + fi_c$field + fi_c$iid + bd * pred_con$dhw +
  vapply(yr_c, yint, numeric(1)) +
  vapply(yr_c, yslp, numeric(1)) * pred_con$dhw

fm1_con <- attach_preds(pred_con, eta_c) |> mutate(era = "contemporary")


## ============================================================
## 3. combine + plot: reefs predicted "High" per year, both eras
## ============================================================

both <- bind_rows(st_drop_geometry(fm1_hind),
                  st_drop_geometry(fm1_con))

nreefs <- both$LABEL_ID |> unique() |>  length()




bleaching_years <- c(1998, 2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025)

counts <- both |>
  filter(cat_mode %in% c("Very high")) |>
  count(year, era, name = "n")


n_reef_CI <- counts |>
  filter(!year %in% bleaching_years) |>
  pull(n) |>
  t.test(conf.level = 0.95) |>
  pluck("conf.int")


counts_filtered <- counts |>
  filter(n >  n_reef_CI[2]) |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(paste0(year,"-01-01"))) |>
  mutate(proportion = (n/nreefs)*100)

count_names <- both |>
  filter(cat_mode %in% c("Very high")) |>
  count(year, era, name = "n") |>
  mutate(proportion = (n/nreefs)*100) |>
  filter(n > n_reef_CI[2]) |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(paste0(year,"-01-01"))) |>
  mutate(linecolor=c("coral3","coral3","coral3","#2166AC","#2166AC", "coral3", "coral3", "coral3","#2166AC", "coral3", "#2166AC","coral3", "#2166AC", "coral3", "#2166AC")) |> 
  mutate( plot_datetime = as.POSIXct(date, tz = "UTC"))


predicted_bleaching <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_segment(
    data = count_names |>
      mutate(
       
        linecolor = "grey50"
      ),
    aes(
      x = plot_datetime,
      xend = plot_datetime,
      y = n,
      yend = 4500,
      colour = linecolor
    ),
    linewidth = 1,
    alpha = 0.3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_col(
    data = counts_filtered |>
      mutate(plot_datetime = as.POSIXct(date, tz = "UTC")),
    aes(plot_datetime, n, fill = n),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = count_names |>
      mutate(plot_datetime = as.POSIXct(date, tz = "UTC")),
    aes(plot_datetime, n, label = year),
    size = 2.5,
    angle = 90,
    vjust = 0.5,
    hjust = -0.1
  ) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "Reds",
    direction = 1
  ) +
  scale_colour_identity() +
  scale_x_datetime(
    limits = as.POSIXct(c("1940-01-01", "2030-01-01"), tz = "UTC"),
    breaks = as.POSIXct(
      seq(
        as.Date("1940-01-01"),
        as.Date("2020-01-01"),
        by = "10 years"
      ),
      tz = "UTC"
    ),
    date_labels = "%Y",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 4500),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Reefs predicted ≥ High (modal class)"
  ) +
  theme(
  #  axis.text.x = element_blank(),
   # axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  ) + ylab("Year")

## ============================================================
## 4. add ENSO
## ============================================================



library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(slider)

# nino34_anomaly_raw <- read.csv(
#   "https://psl.noaa.gov/data/timeseries/month/data/nino34.long.anom.csv"
# ) |>
#   rename(date = 1, anomaly = 2) |>
#   mutate(
#     date = as.Date(date),
#     anomaly = ifelse(anomaly == -9999.00, NA_real_, anomaly)
#   )

#write.csv(nino34_anomaly_raw, "~/GBR-dhw/datasets/nino34_anomaly_raw.csv")

nino34_anomaly_raw <- read.csv("~/GBR-dhw/datasets/nino34_anomaly_raw.csv")


nino34_anomaly_timeseries <- nino34_anomaly_raw |>
  filter(date >= as.Date("1939-01-01")) |>
  arrange(date) |>
  mutate(
    year = year(date),
    month = month(date),

    summer_year = case_when(
      month %in% c(11, 12) ~ year,
      month %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ year - 1L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),

    anomaly_3mo = slide_dbl(
      anomaly,
      mean,
      .before = 1,
      .after = 1,
      .complete = TRUE,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),

    anomaly_3mo_pos = pmax(anomaly_3mo, 0),
    anomaly_3mo_neg = pmin(anomaly_3mo, 0)
  )

nino34_ndjfm <- nino34_anomaly_timeseries |>
  filter(month %in% c(11, 12, 1, 2, 3)) |>
  group_by(summer_year) |>
  summarise(
    ndjfm_anomaly = mean(anomaly, na.rm = TRUE),
    ndjfm_anomaly_3mo = mean(anomaly_3mo, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_months = sum(!is.na(anomaly)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(n_months == 5)

event_dates <- as.Date(c(
  "1964-01-01", "1970-01-01", "1973-01-01", "1982-01-01", "1986-01-01",
  "1987-01-01", "1992-01-01", "1998-01-01", "2002-01-01", "2016-01-01",
  "2017-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2022-01-01", "2024-01-01", "2025-01-01"
))

nino34_anomaly_timeseries_events <- tibble(event_date = event_dates) |>
  mutate(
    event_year = year(event_date),
    summer_year = event_year - 1L,
    plot_date = as.Date(paste0(event_year, "-01-15"))
  ) |>
  left_join(
    nino34_ndjfm,
    by = "summer_year"
  )

nino34_anomaly_timeseries <- nino34_anomaly_raw |>
  filter(date >= as.Date("1942-02-01")) |>
  arrange(date) |>
  mutate(
    year = year(date),
    month = month(date),

    summer_year = case_when(
      month %in% c(11, 12) ~ year,
      month %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ year - 1L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),

    anomaly_3mo = slide_dbl(
      anomaly,
      mean,
      .before = 1,
      .after = 1,
      .complete = TRUE,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),

    anomaly_3mo_pos = pmax(anomaly_3mo, 0),
    anomaly_3mo_neg = pmin(anomaly_3mo, 0)
  )

nino34_ndjfm <- nino34_anomaly_timeseries |>
  filter(month %in% c(11, 12, 1, 2, 3)) |>
  group_by(summer_year) |>
  summarise(
    ndjfm_anomaly = mean(anomaly, na.rm = TRUE),
    ndjfm_anomaly_3mo = mean(anomaly_3mo, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_months = sum(!is.na(anomaly)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(n_months == 5)



nino34_ndjfm_plot <- nino34_anomaly_timeseries |>
  filter(month %in% c(11, 12, 1, 2, 3)) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(
    ndjfm_anomaly = mean(anomaly, na.rm = TRUE),
    ndjfm_anomaly_3mo = mean(anomaly_3mo, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_months = sum(!is.na(anomaly)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(n_months == 5)

event_dates <- as.Date(c(
  "1964-01-01", "1970-01-01", "1973-01-01", "1982-01-01", "1986-01-01",
  "1987-01-01", "1992-01-01", "1998-01-01", "2002-01-01", "2016-01-01",
  "2017-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2022-01-01", "2024-01-01", "2025-01-01"
))

nino34_anomaly_timeseries_events <- tibble(event_date = event_dates) |>
  mutate(
    event_year = year(event_date),
    summer_year = event_year - 1L,
    plot_date = as.Date(paste0(event_year, "-01-15"))
  ) |>
  left_join(
    nino34_ndjfm,
    by = "summer_year"
  ) |>
  mutate(linecolor = ifelse(ndjfm_anomaly < 0, "#2166AC", "coral3")) |> 
  mutate(plot_datetime = as.POSIXct(plot_date, tz = "UTC"))


historical_34 <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_area(
    data = nino34_anomaly_timeseries,
    aes(as.POSIXct(date, tz = "UTC"), anomaly_3mo_pos),
    fill = "#B2182B",
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.04
  ) +
  geom_area(
    data = nino34_anomaly_timeseries,
    aes(as.POSIXct(date, tz = "UTC"), anomaly_3mo_neg),
    fill = "#2166AC",
    alpha = 0.5,
    linewidth = 0.04
  ) +
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = -Inf,
    xmax = Inf,
    ymin = -0.8,
    ymax = 0.8,
    fill = "white",
    alpha = 0.65
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = nino34_anomaly_timeseries_events,
    aes(
      x = plot_date,
      xend = plot_date,
      y = ndjfm_anomaly,
      yend = -Inf,
      colour = linecolor
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    alpha = 0.4,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = c(-0.8, 0.8),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.35
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = nino34_anomaly_timeseries,
    aes(as.POSIXct(date, tz = "UTC"), anomaly_3mo),
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = nino34_anomaly_timeseries_events,
    aes(plot_datetime, ndjfm_anomaly, fill = ndjfm_anomaly),
    size = 3,
    shape = 21,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = nino34_anomaly_timeseries_events,
    aes(plot_datetime, ndjfm_anomaly + 0.4, label = event_year),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166AC",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#B2182B",
    midpoint = 0,
    name = "NDJFM\nNiño 3.4"
  ) +
  scale_colour_identity() +
  scale_x_datetime(
    limits = as.POSIXct(c("1940-01-01", "2030-01-01"), tz = "UTC"),
    breaks = as.POSIXct(
      seq(
        as.Date("1940-01-01"),
        as.Date("2020-01-01"),
        by = "10 years"
      ),
      tz = "UTC"
    ),
    date_labels = "%Y",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-2.5, 3)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Niño 3.4 anomaly (°C)"
    # title = "Niño 3.4 anomaly",
    # subtitle = "Line/area = centred 3-month running mean; dashed lines = BoM ±0.8 °C threshold; points = NDJFM austral-summer mean aligned to event year"
  ) +
  theme(
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
      )




library(tidyverse)
library(sf)

event_years <- c(
  1964, 1970, 1973, 1982, 1986, 1987, 1992, 1998, 2002,
  2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025
)

combined_sf <- fm1_hind_lat_range_prob_classes <-
  bind_rows((fm1_hind),
            (fm1_con))
# 
# ggplot() + theme_bw() +
#   geom_sf(data = combined_sf |>
#             filter(year %in% event_years) |>
#             filter(cat_mode %in% c("High", "Very high")) |>
#             st_centroid(),
#           shp=21, alpha=0.5, stroke=0.05,fill="coral3") +
#   facet_wrap(~year, nrow=2)

fm1_hind_lat_range_prob_classes <-
  combined_sf |>
  filter(year %in% event_years) |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  mutate(
    centroid = st_point_on_surface(geometry),
    lon = st_coordinates(centroid)[, 1],
    lat = st_coordinates(centroid)[, 2]
  ) |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`P(>=High)`, `P(VeryHigh)`),
    names_to = "threshold",
    values_to = "probability"
  ) |>
  filter(probability > 0.5) |>
  group_by(year, threshold) |>
  summarise(
    n_reefs = n(),
    min_lat = min(lat, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_lat = max(lat, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_lon = min(lon, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_lon = max(lon, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_lat = mean(lat, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_lat = median(lat, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 
# fm1_hind_lat_range_prob_classes |>
#   mutate(year = factor(year, levels = event_years)) |>
#   ggplot() +
#   theme_bw() +
#   geom_segment(
#     aes(
#       x = year,
#       xend = year,
#       y = min_lat,
#       yend = max_lat
#     ),
#     linewidth = 0.7,
#     colour = "grey40"
#   ) +
#   geom_point(
#     aes(year, min_lat),
#     size = 2.5,
#     shape = 21,
#     fill = "#2166AC"
#   ) +
#   geom_point(
#     aes(year, max_lat),
#     size = 2.5,
#     shape = 21,
#     fill = "#B2182B"
#   ) +
#   geom_point(
#     aes(year, median_lat),
#     size = 2.5,
#     shape = 21,
#     fill = "white"
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     x = NULL,
#     y = "Latitude",
#     title = "Latitudinal extent of predicted bleaching",
#     subtitle = "Range = min–max reef latitude; white point = median latitude"
#   ) +
#   theme(
#     panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
#     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5,size = rel(0.8), hjust = 1),
#   axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
#   axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
#   axis.text.y  = element_text(size = rel(0.8))
# )
#   



min_n_per_year_lat <- 20
lat_band_width <- 1

fm1_hind_lat_ridge <- combined_sf |>
  filter(year %in% event_years) |>
  filter(`P(>=High)` > 0.5) |>
  st_transform(4326) |>
  mutate(
    centroid = st_point_on_surface(geometry),
    lat = st_coordinates(centroid)[, 2],
    lat_band = round(lat / lat_band_width) * lat_band_width,
    year = factor(year, levels = event_years)
  ) |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  add_count(year, lat_band, name = "n_year_lat") |>
  filter(n_year_lat >= min_n_per_year_lat)

lat_plot <- fm1_hind_lat_ridge |>
  ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  ggridges::geom_density_ridges(
    aes(
      x = lat,
      y = year,
      height = after_stat(count),
      group = year,
      colour = year
    ),
    fill = "transparent",
    stat = "density",
    show.legend = FALSE,
    bandwidth = 0.25,
    scale = 2,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    rel_min_height = 0.005
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Latitude",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, size = rel(0.8), hjust = 1)
  ) +
  coord_flip()



lat_plot <- fm1_hind_lat_ridge |>
  ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  ggridges::geom_density_ridges(
    aes(
      x = lat,
      y = year,
      height = after_stat(count),
      group = year,
      color = year
    ),
    fill="transparent",
    stat = "density",
    show.legend=FALSE,
    bandwidth = 0.25,
    scale = 2,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    rel_min_height = 0.005
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Latitude",
    y = NULL,
    #title = "Latitudinal distribution of predicted bleaching",
    #subtitle = "Smoothed density height scaled by reef count per year"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)

  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = rev(colorRampPalette(
      c(
        "#A50026",
        "#D73027",
        "#F46D43",
        "#FDAE61",
        "#E6B800",
        "#CC9A00",
        "#B8860B",
        "#66BD63",
        "#ABDDA4",
        "#E0F3F8",
        "#ABD9E9",
        "#74ADD1",
        "#4575B4",
        "#313695"
      )
    )(16))
  )




# fixed effect summary:
# add 1998 = 0 reference, join to anomalies, then weighted regression
yr_int <- fm1$summary.fixed[grep("^year", rownames(fm1$summary.fixed)),
                            c("mean","sd")]
yr_df <- yr_int |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("term") |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(sub("year", "", term))) |>
  bind_rows(tibble(term = "year1998", mean = 0, sd = NA, year = 1998)) |>
  left_join(nino34_anomaly_timeseries_events |>
              select(year = event_year, ndjfm_anomaly),
            by = "year") |>
  arrange(year)


event_severity <- yr_df |>
  mutate(enso = if_else(ndjfm_anomaly > 0, "El Niño / positive", "neutral–La Niña")) |>
  group_by(enso) |>
  summarise(events = paste(year, collapse = ", "),
            median_severity = median(mean),
            range = paste(round(range(mean),1), collapse = "–"))

fit_df <- yr_df |> filter(!is.na(sd))          # drop 1998 (no sd) for weighted fit
m <- lm(mean ~ ndjfm_anomaly, data = fit_df, weights = 1/sd^2)
#summary(m)
# slope +0.48 per unit anomaly (95% CI roughly −1.44 to +2.39, i.e. 0.4751 ± 2.571 × 0.7457), p = 0.55, R² = 0.075. 

```

#### Figure 3


```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: false
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-cap: |
#|   **Figure 3 | Coral bleaching extent on the Great Barrier Reef in relation to
#|   El Niño–Southern Oscillation state (1940–2025).**
#|   (**A**) Monthly Niño 3.4 sea-surface temperature anomaly (°C), 1940–2025,
#|   with positive (warm, red) and negative (cool, blue) phases shaded. Dashed
#|   horizontal lines mark the conventional ±0.8 °C El Niño and La Niña
#|   thresholds. Filled circles denote the years referenced elsewhere in the
#|   figure, positioned at each year's peak anomaly and coloured by that anomaly;
#|   labelled years comprise the eight mass-bleaching events analysed here (1998,
#|   2002, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2025) together with earlier reference
#|   years. (**B**) Number of reefs predicted to reach high bleaching for each
#|   labelled year, coloured by the corresponding Niño 3.4 anomaly. Predicted
#|   bleaching extent is low and episodic through the twentieth century and
#|   increases sharply from 2016 onward, with the largest extents in the most
#|   recent events. (**C**) Latitudinal profiles of predicted bleaching for each
#|   year, arranged left to right in chronological order; each curve shows the
#|   distribution across latitude from the far northern Great Barrier Reef (−10°)
#|   to the southern reef (−25°), coloured by year. The northward concentration
#|   of bleaching in several events and its progressive southward expansion in
#|   recent years are both apparent. (**D**) Predicted number of reefs bleaching
#|   against the boreal-winter (NDJFM) Niño 3.4 anomaly for each year; point size
#|   scales with bleaching extent and colour with anomaly. Dashed vertical lines
#|   mark the ±0.8 °C ENSO thresholds. Recent high-extent events (2016, 2024)
#|   fall on the warm, El Niño side, whereas the extensive 2020, 2022 and 2025
#|   events occurred under neutral-to-La Niña conditions, indicating that severe
#|   reef-wide bleaching is no longer confined to El Niño years.

tmp <- counts_filtered |> left_join(
  nino34_ndjfm |> rename(year = summer_year))
  


enso <- counts_filtered |> left_join(
  nino34_anomaly_timeseries_events |> rename(year = event_year)) |>
ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_point(
    aes(ndjfm_anomaly, n, fill = ndjfm_anomaly, size = n),
    shape = 21,
    colour = "black",
    alpha = 0.8,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    aes(ndjfm_anomaly, n, label = year),
    size = 2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.8, 0.8), linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,4000)) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(0.5, 8)) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette="RdBu") +
  xlab("El Niño 3.4 anomaly") +
  ylab("n reefs") +
  theme(
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
  axis.text.x  = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
  axis.text.y  = element_text(size = rel(0.8))
)



Figure_history <- ((historical_34 / predicted_bleaching + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))) |
  (lat_plot/enso + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1)))) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 0.3)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A", tag_prefix = "(", tag_suffix = ")") &
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(size = rel(0.85))
  )
```
